S20 -- THE DEFENSIVE COLUMNS ARE WIRED FOR PERMISSION BUT NOT FOR DRIVE (v14 Expression Phase B+, RULED: ONE finding, register do-not-build; it is a defensive-circuit question, not an expression one). The implementer's principle, recorded verbatim: 'DISINHIBITION ONLY PERMITS; SOMETHING MUST DRIVE.' We built the local-GABA gate architecture beautifully onto columns that have nothing to release. (a) vlPAG (freezing) reads 0.000 in EVERY condition. Afferents: vlPAG-GABA (GABA-A, -), VMH (+), DRN (5-HT1A, -). Its ONLY excitatory afferent is VMH -- and VMH is EXCITATION-UNAFFERENTED: VMH afferents are MPOA (GABA-A, -) and MeA (fallback). So CeA -| vlPAG-GABA -| vlPAG disinhibits a column with no drive. FOURTH instance of the unafferented-hub pattern and the sharpest variant: wired ONLY in the sign that cannot start anything. ⚠️ IMMEDIATE SUSPECT (check FIRST, may be the whole answer): MeA->VMH is on the TRANSMITTER FALLBACK (dominant_receptor: None) while MeA->VMHvl is AMPA. Same source, two targets, one decided and one not. MeA is heterogeneous (glutamatergic AND GABAergic) -- the fallback may be silently INVERTING the one edge that would drive the freezing column. GROUND IT; do not guess, do not assume symmetry with VMHvl. (b) dPAG-GABA's own missing release (Phase A registered): the gate runs ~2x observed and directionally wrong because DRN->dPAG-GABA is its only input -- a candidate missing inhibitory input to the gate. (a) and (b) are ONE finding: the defensive columns have their gates but not their drivers. ONE pass, when reached. NB vlPAG->NRA is NOT an error -- it is GROUNDED AND DORMANT (like PBN->LC): it will carry when the column has drive.

CONSTRUCT-VALIDITY CATCH #4 (v14 Expression Phase B+, the worst of the arc, RULED recorded). The facial route's only amygdala access ran BA -> pSTS -> rSMG-TPJ -> aIns -> dACC -> NuFac -- i.e. THROUGH pSTS AND rSMG-TPJ, which ARE the COGNITIVE_MENTALIZING set, the network the CU manipulation SPARES. Left unbuilt (no direct limbic BA->dACC), a CU agent's face would have KEPT MOVING via the spared cognitive route -- the model would have produced the OPPOSITE of the psychopathy expression signature (blunted mimicry is the finding; intact mimicry is what the wiring gave), BY CONSTRUCTION, from a routing nobody chose. Nothing was aimed; the artifact was in the TOPOLOGY. This is why construct validity is a SEPARATE standing check and not a corollary of 'no result is a target': no-result-is-a-target guards against aiming; this guards against topology that aims for you. (The earlier claim 'nothing from the amygdala reaches the face by ANY path' was too strong -- the indirect cognitive path existed; the correction STRENGTHENS the ruling.)

GATE TAXONOMY -- TWO KINDS OF LOCAL-INHIBITORY GATE, and they are grounded DIFFERENTLY (v14 Expression Phase B+, RULED: record it; it governs the eight cortical brakes still to come). (1) PACEMAKER GATES (LC baseline/setpoint 0.15; dPAG-GABA 0.19): fire action potentials TONICALLY UNDER SYNAPTIC BLOCKADE -- an intrinsic rate EXISTS -> GROUND THE RATE from the electrophysiology -> it is a PAIRED SETPOINT DEVIANT (principle 10). (2) RECURRENT E-I GATES (the cortical interneurons: dlPFC-GABA, vmPFC-GABA, dACC-GABA -- all baseline 0.05 / setpoint 0.1, scaffold): neocortical PV+ fast-spiking cells are NOT autonomous pacemakers; under synaptic isolation they are SILENT, and their high in-vivo rate is synaptically DRIVEN feedback. NO INTRINSIC RATE EXISTS -> the grounded content is the LOOP (the recurrent E-I topology, Ferguson & Gao 2018), NOT a rate -> baseline STAYS SCAFFOLD, and the circuit is CORRECTLY NOT a setpoint deviant. DO NOT FORCE A RATE ONTO AN E-I GATE. When the remaining eight cortical brakes are built, RECURRENT-E-I IS THE DEFAULT, not the exception; forcing a pacemaker rate would invent electrophysiology the cell type does not have. Verify per gate which kind it is (does it fire under synaptic blockade?) before grounding.

PRINCIPLE 6 REFINED -- GROUND THE RELATION, NOT ALWAYS A VALUE (v14 Expression Phase B+, RULED). P6 was 'ground a value only where it is groundable in isolation', which ASSUMED the thing to ground is a VALUE. dACC-GABA shows the refinement: sometimes what is groundable in isolation is a RELATION (a topology), NOT a value -- and then the honest move is to GROUND THE RELATION AND LEAVE THE VALUE SCAFFOLD. dACC-GABA grounds the recurrent E-I LOOP (Ferguson & Gao) and DECLARES ITS RATE SCAFFOLD. That is MORE honest than a grounded number would have been: a grounded number here would have been an invented pacemaker rate. The silence-the-element test (principle 1) then carries the mechanism without any grounded rate at all: the loop pulls dlPFC off ceiling; lesion the gate and it runs hot. A grounded RELATION + a scaffold VALUE is a complete, honest element.

S21 -- THE CAPACITY MEASURE IS ABSENT, and it is a DISTINCT CONSTRUCT from engagement (v14 Expression Phase B+, RULED as its own construct pass). test_environment_differentially_engages_the_executive (xfailed) exposed it: the substrate correctly shows adversity RAISES executive ENGAGEMENT (harsh -> more conflict -> dACC monitoring -> dlPFC control recruitment via the grounded dACC->dlPFC edge, Botvinick). But the literature expects adversity to ALSO LOWER CAPACITY (the control an agent CAN exert), and we do not measure that at all. CAPACITY IS NOT A SMALL VARIANT OF ENGAGEMENT -- it is a different construct, and no read-out set summing ACTIVATION can separate it from demand: a passive read-out conflates capacity with demand BY CONSTRUCTION. DESIGN CONSTRAINT (named in the ruling): capacity must be probed at MATCHED DEMAND -- present the SAME challenge to warm- and harsh-reared agents and measure the control EXERTED. Its own pass, when reached. Do NOT satisfy the xfailed test by inverting engagement to harsh>warm -- that manufactures the opposite finding.

S22 -- THE MODALITY DISSOCIATION IS ARCHITECTURAL (guaranteed by an ABSENCE), NOT COMPETITIVE -- and it is a PHASE D MEASUREMENT, not a Phase C claim (v14 Expression Phase C, RULED). 'Separation drives the voice; pain drives the face' fell out of two grounded edges landing on different limbs (emergent -- nobody wired sep->voice). BUT no PAG column reaches the face by ANY path (vlPAG/dPAG/PAG-PANIC -> NuFac: NO PATH); the face is reachable ONLY via dACC. So the two drives are on SEPARATE LIMBS WITH NO CROSSTALK -- the dissociation is currently GUARANTEED BY AN ABSENCE, which is a WEAKER claim than a dissociation that survives contact. STATE IT THAT WAY; do NOT let it harden into 'the model shows a modality dissociation.' THE TEST ALREADY EXISTS: Phase D's grounded cortico-bulbar route reaches BOTH effectors (face, mouth, tongue, larynx, pharynx), so Phase D INTRODUCES THE CROSSTALK. If the dissociation survives that, it becomes a REAL finding rather than an architectural one. Register as a Phase D measurement.

METHOD NOTE -- REACHABILITY IS TRIVIALLY TRUE IN A RECURRENT GRAPH (v14 Expression Phase C, the implementer's 4th self-correction this arc, recorded). A depth-first 'can source X reach effector Y' probe in a 226-edge recurrent connectome returns whatever branch it tried first (e.g. BA -> ... -> AdrenalCortex -> HPCv -> LA -> ... -> NuFac). The question worth asking is NEVER 'is there a path' -- it is 'is there a SHORT FUNCTIONAL one.' Absences, however, survive a bad probe: vlPAG/dPAG/PAG-PANIC -> NuFac genuinely have NO path (S22). Use shortest-path + weight, not reachability, for functional-route claims.

BUILD-DISCIPLINE PATTERN -- WHEN A NEW ELEMENT HAS A STRUCTURAL TWIN, INHERIT ITS BANDS BYTE-FOR-BYTE AND SAY SO (v14 Expression Phase B+/dACC-GABA, RULED: record the pattern). dACC-GABA's loop bands (dACC->dACC-GABA moderate/AMPA; dACC-GABA->dACC moderate-strong/GABA-A) are BYTE-IDENTICAL to dlPFC-GABA's. That is not tidiness -- it is why the result (the brake reducing the divergence 0.0755->0.0534) is trustworthy: the obvious objection is 'you chose a strength that made the number fall', and the answer is now STRUCTURAL not rhetorical -- the bands were INHERITED from the twin, not selected, so there was NO DEGREE OF FREEDOM in which to tune. The strongest form of the anti-tuning discipline: not 'we didn't tune it' but 'there was no knob to tune.' Applies to any new element with an established structural twin.

S23 -- IN-PROPRIO IS DECLARED AND UNWIRED (v14 Phase C, found while checking the face-channel; RULED register, do NOT build). The proprioceptive input channel exists as an id but has ZERO edges. Master S3.2: proprioception supplies 'the efference-copy comparison behind AGENCY' -- so THAT MECHANISM DOES NOT EXIST; the channel is a name. FIFTH instance of the unafferented/unefferented pattern and the FIRST on the INPUT side (PVN-OT unafferented; LC unafferented; the emotional motor system unefferented; the defensive columns wired for permission not drive; now a sense with nothing downstream). PHASE-D-RELEVANT: the model's first motor output (Phase D) would have NO proprioceptive return -- an OPEN LOOP. Phase D's suppressor is FEEDFORWARD and does not need it, so build proceeds; BUT no claim about agency, effort, or self-generated movement is available until IN-PROPRIO lands, and Phase D is the phase that makes someone want to make one.

S24 -- THE CONTAGION LOOP IS DAMPED, NOT SATURATED (v14 Phase C, the 4th and last per-edge-phasic item, RESOLVED). The registered worry (0aa9285) was that the loop was damped ONLY by the display's phasic adaptation -- now correctly gone (S-display, effector output above FIXED rest). NAMED: the peak 0.952 is a MOMENTARY transient; the tail (later-portion) MEAN max-activation settles to 0.67-0.72, well below the 0.95 saturation reference -- so it is a BOUND touched once, not a loop pinned at its bound. The damping is LOOP-GAIN < 1, and strongly so: the honest effector display is weak (face ~0.067), the perception-channel weights are < 1 (face_like->SC-Pv moderate; A1-belt->LA low), and the perceiver's own homeostasis attenuates -- so a bearer's display of 0.067 returns a perceiver display of ~0 (perceiver CeA moves only +0.029). Loop gain << 1 -> converges. THE 4th PHASIC ITEM IS SETTLED: contagion is damped by real dynamics (loop attenuation), NOT the phasic trick and NOT saturation. Per-edge-phasic register now 4 of 4: teaching PHASIC (bursts teach), CeA->LC PHASIC/adapting (CRF adapts), display NOT phasic (effector output is its activation), contagion DAMPED-BY-LOOP-GAIN (not phasic). Caveat: measured in a nurture-dominant Arena; the loop is weakly EXERCISED there, but the loop GAIN < 1 holds independently of how hard it is driven.

S25 -- NuFac UPPER/LOWER SPLIT: the highest-value split for the thesis (v14 Phase D, RULED register, do not act; queue behind dPAG dl/l and dACC cognitive/motor, but FLAG as the most valuable of the three). Gross's suppression signature is VISIBLE DISPLAY down while MUSCLE ACTIVITY and autonomic cost go UP -- and with ONE NuFac scalar, 'visible display' and 'muscle activity' are the SAME number, so that signature cannot be represented. The grain is named by the literature: orbicularis oculi (UPPER face) is LESS subject to voluntary control and is the DUCHENNE marker -- which is why a posed smile is detectable. 'Displays without feeling' is, anatomically, a LOWER-face-without-upper-face signature. Splitting NuFac -> NuFac-upper / NuFac-lower is what would make the facial psychopathy signature EXPRESSIBLE at all.

S26 -- THE SUBCORTICAL INVOLUNTARY FACIAL ROUTE IS ABSENT (v14 Phase D, RULED register + DISCLOSE against any dual-pathway claim). The clinical account: a SECOND supranuclear pathway to the facial nucleus -- the limbic/extrapyramidal route via basal ganglia, thalamus and brainstem -- controls involuntary facial movement; its selective damage leaves volitional movement intact while ABOLISHING emotional expression (Parkinson's damages it -> the masked face). That is the lesion proof. We have ONLY the CORTICAL involuntary route (BA->dACC/M3->NuFac). So our 'involuntary' pathway is the cortical HALF of a two-part system. The dual-pathway dissociation measured in Phase D (involuntary blunted under AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY throttle, volitional spared) rides on the cortical half only -- DISCLOSE this against the result.

S27 -- REAPPRAISAL vs EXPRESSIVE SUPPRESSION EMERGED, UNCODED (v14 Phase D, ★ HEADLINE FINDING, not a bonus (RULED)). The model spontaneously distinguishes Gross's two regulation strategies, because they run on two different grounded pathways: (1) REAPPRAISAL = vlPFC -> ITC (intercalated cells) -| amygdala -> reduces the FEELING (driving vlPFC drops CeA 0.491->0.228); (2) EXPRESSIVE SUPPRESSION = PMC-l -> PAG-PANIC-GABA -| PAG-PANIC -> reduces the DISPLAY with FEELING UNCHANGED (driving PMC-l drops the cry 53% while CeA holds at 0.491). This is Gross S3 exactly (antecedent-focused reappraisal changes the emotion; response-focused suppression acts on the response, feeling unchanged) -- fallen out of the two pathways, never built. NB engaging the full executive goal (vlPFC) does BOTH (vlPFC drives PMC-l AND ITC); isolating pure expressive suppression means driving PMC-l, the suppression hub, directly. ★ WHY THIS IS THE HEADLINE: Gross's process model -- antecedent-focused (reappraisal) vs response-focused (suppression) regulation -- FELL OUT OF WHERE TWO GROUNDED ROUTES LAND. Nobody coded 'reappraisal changes the emotion, suppression changes only the display.' It fell out of one route reaching the amygdala's inhibitory gate (vlPFC->ITC) and the other reaching the expression column's (PMC-l->PAG-PANIC-GABA). THE DISTINCTION IS THE ANATOMY. A PSYCHOLOGICAL THEORY EMERGED FROM A CONNECTOME -- the strongest demonstration to date that the substrate is a REPRESENTATION, not a restatement. This is what six levels of descent were for.

S28 -- THE AUTONOMIC COST OF SUPPRESSION DOES NOT EMERGE (v14 Phase D, honest gap; register). Gross's third sub-signature is that suppression EFFORT raises sympathetic arousal (HR/SCR rise). Measured: SympOut is UNCHANGED under expressive suppression (0.137->0.137). No mechanism links the suppression effort (PMC-l engagement) to SympOut, so the cost cannot emerge. NOT built (a cost coded at an invented rate is exactly what the discipline forbids); register as the missing arm of the suppression signature. Candidate: an effort/conflict -> autonomic pathway (dACC monitors regulation success, Master S3) -- its own grounded pass. ▲ PRINCIPLE 1 (RULED): a finding that does not emerge is a MISSING PATHWAY. The question is not 'why is the cost absent' but 'WHAT ROUTE CARRIES the autonomic cost of effortful suppression?' Candidate class: effort/cognitive-load -> sympathetic drive (dACC monitors regulation success, Master S3; SympOut EXISTS and is the correct terminal). Its OWN grounding pass -- do NOT build it into Phase D and do NOT claim the cost until the route is grounded.

S29 -- M1-face's OPEN AFFERENT IS A SOCKET, NOT A GAP -- the first hook of the climb back (v14 Phase D, RULED: confirm the PMC-l->M1-face omission, but NOT for the reason I first gave). ⚠️ THE REASON I FIRST GAVE WAS WRONG: 'including PMC-l->M1-face masks voice-down' is omitting REAL anatomy because it changes a result we like -- the BA->dACC-to-'low' failure shape, one step removed. It was surfaced, not acted on, hence a ruling. The omission stands on THREE reasons that survive the result going either way: (1) PMC-l IS LUMPED -- it both SUPPRESSES (lateral premotor -> the faciorespiratory column) and PLANS VOLITIONAL PRODUCTION (premotor->M1); one node, two functions, so driving the suppressor necessarily drives production. In real cortex these are different populations (you can hold your voice without speaking). PMC-l is a SPLIT CANDIDATE (the fourth: dPAG dl/l, dACC cognitive/motor, NuFac upper/lower, now PMC-l). (2) NuAmb-vocal IS LUMPED and the DISPLAY cannot cope -- cry and speech genuinely share the laryngeal motoneurons (that lumping is anatomically CORRECT), but the display reads the motoneuron POOL's activation, so it cannot tell a cry from a sentence. So 'the cry goes up' when PMC-l drives M1-face is the display MISREADING volitional vocalisation as distress -- a read-out limitation of the NuFac-upper/lower class (in reality a cry and a sentence are distinguished ACOUSTICALLY, not by which motoneurons fire). Register. (3) ★ M1-face BEING AFFERENT-LESS IS CORRECT, and it is the real reason. Its afferent is a LEARNED DISPLAY RULE ('smile at the boss', 'don't cry in front of strangers') -- learned by modelling, correction and reinforcement, and living in the REPRESENTATIONAL-MEMORY-EXECUTIVE layer (S2.16: beliefs, attitudes, and the rules that control behaviour through the executive). THAT LAYER IS D3 -- the level we descended FROM. It is not built. So 'posing is a capacity with no spontaneous trigger' is EXACTLY right: the model CAN pose and has no reason to, because the reason is learned one level up. Wiring a trigger now would make posing a coded disposition -- a coded display rule, which the standing rule forbids. An unafferented node nobody noticed is a defect; an unafferented node whose afferent is a NAMED, REGISTERED dependency one level up is a SOCKET. RESOLUTION: M1-face's afferent lands when the representational layer's display rules exist. THE EXPRESSION SYSTEM IS NOW COMPLETE EXCEPT FOR ITS LEARNED TRIGGER -- and the trigger is the thing we descended to get.

S30 -- LC's AFFERENT SET IS INCOMPLETE, and it is RECORDED AS SUCH (v14 D6 fix #2 door 1, RULED -- the actual fix for 'partial completion recorded as completion' is in the RECORDING, not the completion). Built: PGi (the major excitatory afferent). NOT built, named: PrH (blocked on the vestibular/oculomotor system, S31) - preBotC-Cdh9 (with the respiratory pass, S32) - Barrington's nucleus - the PVN dorsal cap - spinal lamina X. ★ THE FAILURE MODE, recorded as a principle: 'LC now has a teaching signal' was TRUE; 'LC is afferented' was NOT. Completing a hub means completing its afferent SET, not adding the edge the current work needs -- and the TELL is the audit's: when ONE afferent carries ~100% of a hub's drive, the hub is not complete, it is minimally viable, and every claim about manipulating that afferent is ENTAILED. NEVER AGAIN record a hub complete when it is not.

S30b -- ★ THE FAILURE MODE RECURSED ONE LEVEL DOWN: PGi's OWN afferent set is INCOMPLETE, and the re-measure PROVES it matters (v14 D6 fix #2 door 1, honest as-found result). Built PGi's INTEROCEPTIVE/pain arm (PBN, NTS, IN-SOMATO:nociception) but NOT its EXTEROCEPTIVE/salience arm -- yet Aston-Jones says PGi integrates 'exteroceptive AND interoceptive... arousal.' CONSEQUENCE, measured: the D6 #2 entailment DISSOLVED under DIRECT PAIN (LC collapse under AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY throttle 72%->37%, PGi fires 0.537 via nociception) but SURVIVES under DISTRESS/VICARIOUS (73%->64%, PGi stays 0.084 -- biological_motion/voice never reach it). ⚠️ THE VICARIOUS CONTEXT IS THE CU STUDY'S CONTEXT -- so door 1 is NOT closed for the purpose that matters: the entailment on VICARIOUS learning survives, because PGi is blind to social distress. NEXT GROUNDED STEP (its own ruling): PGi's exteroceptive/salience arm -- a salience input (candidate SC-Pv-> PGi, the biological-motion/looming salience hub). Do NOT add it unilaterally; surface. Recorded INCOMPLETE, not papered over.

S31 -- PrH (nucleus prepositus hypoglossi): a NAMED, GROUNDED ABSENCE, BLOCKED (v14 D6 fix #2, RULED do NOT build). It IS a major LC afferent (Aston-Jones 1986). But its OWN afferents are the vestibular/oculomotor system, which DOES NOT EXIST in this substrate -- so building PrH would create exactly the unafferented hub we are here to fix: the failure mode, repeated, inside the fix for the failure mode. Blocked on an unbuilt system; build when/if the vestibular-oculomotor system is built.

S32 -- preBotC-Cdh9: a SUBPOPULATION, registered with the respiratory pass (v14 D6 fix #2, RULED do NOT build). Yackle et al. 2017: the mild arousal of a novel environment is driven EXCLUSIVELY by Cdh9/Dbx1 pre-Botzinger neurons projecting to LC. A node called 'preBotC' would LUMP the rhythm generator with the arousal projection -- the TENTH lump (see the lumping register), added knowingly in the pass whose subject is lumping. And the substrate has no oscillation, so the rhythm is not representable regardless. Register the SUBPOPULATION preBotC-Cdh9 (arousal projection only) with the respiratory pass -- one circuit, two registers.

S33 -- ★ AN EXCLUSION IS A CLAIM, and it goes stale like any other (v14 D6 fix #2, a NEW audit category the D6 pass could not catch -- it looked at what is IN the sets, never at whether the reasons for what is OUT are still true). Found while verifying the temperament domains: _TEMPERAMENT_DOMAIN excludes FRUSTRATION with the comment 'FRUSTRATION maps to the attack circuits, which are net-inhibited in v8 (OBS-3), so it has no leverage.' THE PAG SPLIT DESTROYED THAT PREMISE: the v9 keystone flip found the suppression was COLUMN CONFUSION -- CeA inhibits FREEZING (vlPAG), not attack. So FRUSTRATION's exclusion justification is STALE and it MAY now have leverage. THE GENERAL RULE (add to the audit): every exclusion must CARRY a justification, and every justification must be RE-CHECKED when the thing it rests on moves -- especially one justified by a substrate fact, in a substrate we change every commit. Same shape as the six stale-memory errors: a fact true when recorded, false now, never re-read. DIAGNOSE FRUSTRATION in its own small pass; do NOT act here.

S34 -- ★ S18 IS DIRECTIONAL AND ASYMMETRICALLY PROTECTS NEGATIVE FINDINGS, and the completion arc is a MEASURED LAW (v14 D6 fix #2, RULED record). On an INFLATING substrate (S18: artifacts inflate) every effect size is an OVER-estimate -- so an earned NEGATIVE holds A FORTIORI (true value even lower). The divergence earned-negative arc: 0.0755 (saturation) -> 0.0534 (dACC-GABA brake) -> 0.0335 (PGi afferent) -- three grounded completions, three monotone shrinkages on one quantity, PREDICTED BEFORE OBSERVED. The caution that kept the finding open at 0.0755 (above 0.05) now CONFIRMS it at 0.0335 (below). The test (test_divergence_does_not_robustly_emerge) is un-xfailed and now CARRIES A PREDICTION: every remaining grounded completion (8 cortical brakes, vlPAG's drive, PGi's exteroceptive arm) should shrink it further; if any completion makes it GROW, that is the S18 surprise to STOP for, and the test goes red. A pin became an instrument.

S35 -- ★ THE COMPLETION STOPPING RULE (v14 D6 fix #2, RULED). 'Complete the hub' is recursive -- LC completed with one afferent -> PGi specced without asking whether IT would be afferented -> PGi built with one arm (three recursions, each partiality invisible until measured). The stopping rule is NOT anatomical ('complete everything' is the whole brain); it is the MEASUREMENT, taken IN THE CONTEXT THE CLAIM IS MADE IN: complete until NO SINGLE INPUT CARRIES THE CLAIM, and test WHERE THE CLAIM LIVES. The CU study's claim lives in the VICARIOUS context, so door 1 (the AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY->LC entailment) closes when the entailment dissolves under VICARIOUS DISTRESS -- NOT under direct pain (72%->37% looks like a resolution and is not) and NOT on the anatomy looking tidy. 64% under vicarious = door still open; PGi's exteroceptive/salience arm is next (S30b).

S36 -- A RESOLUTION CONDITION NAMES THE QUESTION, NOT THE MECHANISM (v14 D6 fix #2, RULED record). The earned-negative xfail's condition was 'when the cortical brake layer is complete' -- a GUESS about which change would answer the question, wearing a condition's clothes. It fired EARLY (for PGi, not a brake) because the real mechanism was general (any grounded completion = S18). The correct condition was 're-measure after ANY grounded completion; the finding holds while it stays below 0.05.' NB the STRICTNESS saved it: a non-strict xfail would have sat green and silent while three completions confirmed the finding and nobody noticed -- the strict mechanism caught our own bad condition.

S37 -- PGi's EXTEROCEPTIVE ARM: the visual channel is UNRESOLVED, blocked on OUR incompleteness -- NOT 'true by anatomy' (v14 D6 fix #2 door 1; the reviewer WITHDREW the true-by-anatomy verdict, and I refine the mechanism). Built dPAG->PGi (cited, Van Bockstaele 1993). The route IN-VIS:biological_motion -> SC-Pv -> dPAG -> PGi -> LC exists but the visual entailment is UNCHANGED at 64%. REVIEWER'S CORRECTION: this is not dPAG 'declining to relay observation' -- it is a DORMANT edge (the vlPAG->NRA class), blocked on the registered defensive-drive gap, so we CANNOT yet rule the channel. MY REFINEMENT (measured): dPAG is NOT inoperable -- its ATTACK function FIRES (provocation -> VMHvl -> dPAG at 0.85 crosses the gate; dPAG=0.365, and dPAG->PGi then carries it, PGi 0.084->0.208). What is blocked is the FLIGHT drive: SC-Pv -> dPAG (0.50, the weak Point-1 synapse) cannot cross the UNRELIEVED dPAG-GABA gate (0.85) -- and the gate's RELEASE is missing (Stempel & Evans: VGAT+ activity DECREASES at escape onset; escape is disinhibition; nothing dampens ours). This is the dPAG dl/l LUMP: the lateral/attack drive crosses, the dorsolateral/flight drive does not. So dPAG->PGi carries the observer's own ATTACK-arousal (provocation), NOT observed visual distress; the visual-distress route rides the flight drive and is blocked. CORRECTED VERDICT: visual channel UNRESOLVED, blocked on the dPAG-GABA release + the weak SC-Pv->dPAG synapse (the defensive-drive pass). Do NOT characterise from a partial assembly (principle 6). The convenient answer is not always an edge -- it can be a tidy VERDICT ('it's the anatomy, stop'), and that one was premature in the direction of closing. ▲▲ VERDICT REINSTATED (defensive-drive ruling, now MEASURED not inferred): the escape gate is SURMOUNTABLE (~SC-Pv 0.7), observation lands at 0.584 (sub-escape), and the threshold is GROUNDED electrophysiology (dPAG-GABA 0.19). So the VISUAL channel is TRUE BY ANATOMY -- 'observed distress does not reach arousal via the escape column' is a grounded, measured, EMERGENT fact, not a gap (dPAG is escape-gated; observation is correctly sub-escape; a grounded rate produced it). AUDITORY channel still live (injection depth, S42). My two prior verdicts were both INFERRED; the sweep settled it -- S35 (the stopping rule is the measurement) applied to a verdict.

S38 -- PGi's AFFERENT SET recorded INCOMPLETE, remainder NAMED FROM THE CITED LIST (Van Bockstaele, Akaoka & Aston-Jones 1993 -- we now have the list). NOT built: (1) the AUDITORY arm (inferior colliculus + paralemniscal zone -> PGi) -- ⚠️ TWO blocks: AUD-brainstem LUMPS the IC (nodes='cochlear nuclei -> superior olive -> inferior colliculus'; a grain issue), AND the cry BYPASSES it (IN-AUD:voice -> A1-belt directly, Phase C), so IC->PGi would not carry the vocal distress as wired -- pending an auditory-grain pass; (2) the DORSAL COLUMN NUCLEI (the somatosensory exteroceptive arm) -- the model has no spinal/trigeminal relay, so BLOCKED ON AN UNBUILT SYSTEM, like PrH (S31); (3) the paramedian/paraolivary reticular formations, retrofacial PGi, caudal medullary RF. PGi's set is minimally viable, not complete -- named per S30/S35.

S39 -- ★ THE REFUSAL FOUND THE REAL MECHANISM -- the arc's cleanest instance of the rule (v14 D6 fix #2, RULED record). SC-Pv->PGi was four-for-four convenient (built, afferented/passes-S35, carries biological_motion, would dissolve the entailment) and grounded only on a FUNCTION description. Refused it on the GROUNDS, not the outcome. The paper then confirmed SC-Pv is NOT a PGi afferent (the cited set is somatosensory/auditory/autonomic, no visual/collicular) AND named a DIFFERENT edge that does the job and is grounded: dPAG->PGi. Had SC-Pv->PGi been routed, an invented edge would have done the work of a real one, the problem would have looked solved, and the real edge would never have been found. 'Don't route it there because it would work' is not caution -- it is the thing that finds the real mechanism. Same consequence, OPPOSITE EPISTEMICS: we found dPAG->PGi by asking what the paper says is there, not what dissolves the entailment. (That the real edge turned out functionally GATED, S37, is itself the honest anatomy -- a real gate, not a convenient number.)

S40 -- THE ESCAPE GATE IS SURMOUNTABLE; the blocker is the INPUT, not the release (v14 defensive-drive, Half 2 sensitivity sweep -- answer (b), NOT (a)). Sweeping the two scaffold bands (dPAG-GABA->dPAG, SC-Pv->dPAG): a salient SC drive CROSSES at ~SC-Pv 0.7 at the current bands (dPAG 0.12; up to 0.20). So the bands are LOAD-BEARING (principle 2) and directionally-right per the relational grounding (dense inhibition / sparse excitation) -- the 'missing release' is NOT what blocks escape. ⚠️ METHOD NOTE: my FIRST sweep drove IN-VIS:looming, which does NOT reach SC-Pv (its only visual channels are face_like + biological_motion), so SC-Pv stayed 0.13 and nothing crossed -- a NULL from a manipulation that never engaged the mechanism, nearly reported as 'release missing.' Caught by checking the source's channels. THE REAL GAP: the escape-TRIGGER INPUT looming->SC-Pv is MISSING (the model has the gate + relay, not the trigger); and observation (biological_motion -> SC-Pv 0.584) is CORRECTLY sub-threshold (you do not flee from watching -- refines S37: dPAG is escape-gated, observation is correctly sub-escape). Register: (i) looming->SC-Pv missing; (ii) dPAG-GABA's release (Stempel's VGAT+ dip) UNRESOLVED -- the paper does not name its source, do not invent it.

S41 -- MeA is the TENTH lump and VMH the ELEVENTH, and both MeA->VMH edges need re-grounding as ONE keystone-bound pass (v14 defensive-drive, Half 1; RULED diagnose-do-not-build). Grounded: MeApv (GLUTAMATERGIC) -> VMHdm (our VMH, predator-defence) vs MePD (GABAergic) -> VMHvl (reproduction/aggression) -- opposite transmitters, opposite targets, opposite functions, one node; the seed's transmitter field 'GABA/glutamate' IS the lump, and the fallback resolved it by coin-flip (picked GABA -> killed VMH's drive -> vlPAG dead). VMH is also lumped: core (glutamatergic, projecting) + shell (GABAergic, inhibits core). CONSEQUENCES: (1) MeA->VMH should be GLUTAMATERGIC (+) -- the grounded fix for vlPAG's missing drive, available. (2) MeA->VMHvl (currently AMPA, and it FEEDS THE AGGRESSION KEYSTONE) is probably WRONG: MePD->VMH is GABAergic and the shell inhibits the core, so the aggression route is very likely MePD(GABA)->VMH shell(GABA)->disinhibits the core -- the CeA->vlPAG-GABA disinhibition architecture. Our AMPA = right OUTCOME by the wrong MECHANISM, the v9 keystone-flip story on a keystone edge. Fixing one sign inside a lump is how the CeA->PAG artifact happened -- bring the split + both edges together. Q2 (what drives vlPAG once disinhibited) waits: the VMH=0.116 counterfactual was measured on the wrong (lumped) node.

S42 -- THE INPUT SURFACE HAS GAPS (catalogue-vs-channels audit; mechanical, report-only). 14 cited innate reinforcers vs 23 wired channels; a missing channel is a NULL not an error, so each gap is a mechanism the model cites and CANNOT exercise: PB-LOOMING (no IN-VIS:looming -- the escape TRIGGER; SC-Pv->dPAG cites it; the escape circuit has never seen a real escape cue); PB-STARTLE (COCH is COMPLETELY unafferented + the only IN-AUD is voice->A1-belt/cortex -> startle dead at the input); PR-SALT (no IN-GUST:salt); PR-HOMEOSTATIC (thirst missing; hunger/satiety/CO2 wired); + IN-PROPRIO (S23). ★★ THE AUDITORY INPUT SURFACE IS BROKEN: COCH (brainstem auditory entry) has NO afferents at all and there is no bare IN-AUD -- the whole subcortical pathway COCH->AUD-brainstem(IC)->SC-Pv/MGN is unafferented. ONE gap explains THREE findings: startle can't fire, the cry can't reach SC-Pv, the cry can't reach the IC (Phase C injection-depth generalised: not 'the cry enters too high' but 'the subcortical auditory input does not exist'). THE RULE: every cited innate reinforcer must have a working input channel; run this audit before each build.

S43 -- THE LUMP CENSUS (mechanical, report-only; the lump-detector at scale). Detector: dominant_receptor None AND a source whose transmitter field names TWO transmitters (the two-transmitter filter is what makes it a detector -- the fallback is CORRECT for a homogeneous source; the raw 161/236=68% fallback count is not the finding). 16 lump-declaring sources found BY QUERY. DANGEROUS subset (subpopulation-lumps, projection sign flips by subpopulation -- the coin-flip can be backwards as MeA->VMH was): MeA, VMH, MPOA, BNST, HYPdm, PVN (hypothalamic/amygdala). Lower-risk (cortical/relay: glut projection + local GABA, fallback probably + but sign is silently ORDER-DEPENDENT on the transmitter field -- 'glutamate/GABA'->+, 'GABA/glutamate'->-): A1-belt, AUD-brainstem, S1, V1, VN, pIns, dlPFC, RET, NAc-shell, PPTg. 11 lumps were found by accident one at a time; this detector finds them by query -- a mechanical pre-computation of the 209-edge audit's wrong-target/missing-element categories. Report, fix nothing. ▲ KNOWN-HAZARDOUS INSTANCES, not candidates (defensive-drive (A) fixed 2 of MeA's 5 efferents; 3 remain on the fallback, same node/field/mechanism that produced a backwards sign TWICE in one pass): (i) MeA->MPOA -- MPOA is in MePD's REPRODUCTIVE target list -> GABAergic; currently signed by typing order. (ii) ⚠️ MeA->BNST CANNOT be right at ANY single sign: MePV -> medial posteroINTERmediate BST (defensive) and MePD -> medial posteroMEDIAL BST (reproductive) -- two projections, opposite subpopulations, opposite transmitters, ONE edge. A LUMP PROJECTING TO A LUMP; no value is correct, the edge is the wrong SHAPE (needs both nodes split, which needs the predator channel, S45). (iii) MeA->ATL-TP (fallback, basis=assumption). These sit at the TOP of the census as demonstrated instances. ▲ TWELFTH LUMP: vlPAG is a FOUR-transmitter node (glutamatergic / GABAergic / SEROTONERGIC / DOPAMINERGIC). The DOPAMINERGIC cells are AUTORHYTHMIC PACEMAKERS (there is a paper on pacemaker frequency in vlPAG/DRN DA neurons) -- so the tonic population that made the Q2 question plausible is REAL but a DIFFERENT cell type than the glut output (target-cell resolution again). ★ UNIQUE: it is the ONLY lump where the two nodes are the SAME TISSUE -- vlPAG CONTAINS the serotonergic+dopaminergic cells that ARE the DR ('vlPAG-DR is often regarded as one area'), so DRN->vlPAG is an edge from a node to a node that CONTAINS it: a GRAIN error, not a sign error (S47).

S44 -- ★ THE FALLBACK SIGN IS SET BY TYPING ORDER -- an accident WITH a sign (v14 defensive-drive, RULED register with the census S43; the most alarming thing in the pass). For a two-transmitter source on the receptor fallback, the sign is the source's PRINCIPAL-transmitter default -- which for 'glutamate/GABA' resolves + and for 'GABA/glutamate' resolves - . So the sign of every fallback edge from a heterogeneous source is determined by WHICH TRANSMITTER THE AUTHOR WROTE FIRST. MeA->VMH's wrong sign (-1, killing vlPAG's drive) was not a coin-flip -- it was TYPING ORDER. 161/236 edges run on the fallback; for the 16 two-transmitter sources (S43), the transmitter field is not a description, it is a SIGN hidden in a string in an order nobody chose deliberately. A default announces itself; this does not. This makes the lump census URGENT not merely useful: the 6 dangerous sources (MeA/VMH/MPOA/BNST/HYPdm/PVN) are 6 signs currently set by punctuation. Report; the fix travels with each source's grain pass.

S45 -- PREDATOR-ODOR CHANNEL GAP + the freezing floor is TRIVIALLY held (v14 defensive-drive (A), built + honest). (i) CHANNEL GAP: MePV (the MeA defensive arm) is defined by its response to PREDATOR ODOR (cat odor), but MeA's afferents are PIR + V-ventral only -- no predator channel (IN-OLF is generic; IN-CONSPEC is attractive_face/formidability/kin). AND (A) fixed only the two edges the diagnostic implicated (MeA->VMH, MeA->VMHvl); THREE efferents remain on the fallback and are now KNOWN-HAZARDOUS (MeA->MPOA GABAergic, MeA->BNST wrong-shape lump->lump, MeA->ATL-TP) -- see S43. (ii) THE FREEZING FLOOR IS TRIVIALLY HELD: the MeA->VMH sign flip revived VMH (0.000->0.116) but vlPAG STILL barely fires (0.008) -- VMH->vlPAG (low-moderate) is too weak. So 'neutral/conspecific -> no freezing' passes, but it passes BECAUSE vlPAG is dead (Q2), NOT because a floor mechanism restrains it -- the v9 lesson exactly ('a floor that has never existed is not a floor that has held'). The floor becomes a REAL test when Q2 (vlPAG's driver / the VMH->vlPAG band) lands. Guard placed, flagged trivial.

S46 -- TWO auditory asymmetries; the RAW one is closed, the FEATURE one blocks door 1's auditory half (v14, RULED register for the door-1-auditory pass -- NOT now). Vision's FEATURES enter SUBCORTICALLY (IN-VIS:face_like, IN-VIS:biological_motion -> SC-Pv) but audition's enters in CORTEX (IN-AUD:voice -> A1-belt). Door 1's auditory half needs TWO things: (a) does the Arena present the cry on the RAW channel? A cry is BOTH a sound and a voice; Phase C presents it on IN-AUD:voice only, so the agent hears a voice without hearing a sound -- if this is the gap the fix is in the ARENA not the seed (a one-line change, the subcortical chain now behind it). Check the visual side for the contrast: is a displayed face presented on IN-VIS as well as IN-VIS:face_like? (b) does the IC exist? AUD-brainstem's function names 'interaural sound localisation' -- the SUPERIOR OLIVE's job -- so IC->PGi may have NO SOURCE NODE (a grain question). ⚠️ (a) ALONE DOES NOT CLOSE DOOR 1: a cry reaching SC-Pv->CeA->LC is still AMYGDALA-OBLIGATE (same route the visual one has). The NON-amygdala route is IC->PGi->LC, so door 1's auditory half needs (b), and (a) only feeds it. Do NOT let (a)'s numbers look like a closure -- that is the direct-pain 37% again. Order: (b) the AUD-brainstem/IC grain FIRST, because (a) is worthless without it.

S47 -- Q2 (vlPAG's drive) IS NOT A BAND FIX -- it is a grounding question with two candidates (v14, diagnosed on the settled node; surface, do NOT band-crank). MEASURED: the VMH->vlPAG band is NOT the blocker -- even at `strong` (0.85) vlPAG reaches only 0.080, below the 0.10 freezing threshold, so cranking it would be the BA->dACC-to-'low' error in reverse (raising a cited edge to force a result). The REAL findings: (a) DRN->vlPAG (5-HT1A, -, moderate) is THE SUPPRESSOR -- silence it (band unchanged) and freezing FIRES (vlPAG=0.108). ⚠️ POSSIBLY MIS-SIGNED: serotonin is classically PASSIVE-COPING (freezing-promoting), and DRN already INHIBITS escape (dPAG, Phase A), so DRN also inhibiting FREEZING (vlPAG) is internally inconsistent -- unless the 5-HT1A-in-vlPAG anxiolytic effect (reduces defensive responses) is the intended one. This is the SAME target-cell/receptor resolution as the Phase A DRN->dPAG case (5-HT1A direct vs 5-HT2A-on-the-gate disinhibition): does DRN PROMOTE or SUPPRESS vlPAG freezing? Grounding needed. (b) vlPAG's AFFERENT SET IS INCOMPLETE -- VMH is the SOLE excitatory afferent (the 'a hub is complete only when its afferent set is' pattern -- LC/PGi again). The medial hypothalamic defensive system -> vlPAG freezing has more arms (dorsal premammillary PMd, mPFC top-down); vlPAG's drive is minimally-viable, not complete. Q2 = ground (a) the DRN->vlPAG serotonergic sign AND/OR (b) complete vlPAG's afferents. Turns the freezing-floor red (S45) real. Do NOT crank VMH->vlPAG. ▲ (a) CLOSED (RULED): DRN->vlPAG (-) is CORRECT. Deakin & Graeff: the DRN has TWO arms with OPPOSITE signs on DIFFERENT targets -- DRN->amygdala FACILITATES fear, DRN->PAG INHIBITS panic (5-HT1A is anxiolytic, inhibiting defensive behaviour). Suppressing BOTH defensive columns is what an anxiolytic does; the facilitation arm lives at the AMYGDALA, not the PAG -- no inconsistency. Silencing it fired freezing, but silencing a CORRECT edge to get a wanted result is the same move as cranking a band -- the lever was found and not pulled. DO NOT TOUCH DRN->vlPAG or VMH->vlPAG (both correct, both levers). ⚠️ GRAIN NOTE (register, do not act): DRN and vlPAG are the SAME TISSUE -- the DR is embedded in the vlPAG and DR^DA neurons extend into it; vlPAG-DR is often regarded as ONE area. The model represents INTRA-regional serotonergic modulation as an INTER-nodal edge -- not resolvable by a sign.

S48 -- ★ THE REAL Q2: is vlPAG-glut TONICALLY ACTIVE? (v14, RULED -- go to the electrophysiology BEFORE adding a driver; I cannot fetch it this session, so SURFACE). Vaughan & Christie: inhibiting vlPAG-GABA 'LEADS TO ACTIVATION of' the glutamatergic projections -- if disinhibition ALONE activates, then 'disinhibition only permits; something must drive' is FALSE for vlPAG, and vlPAG-glut is a PACEMAKER-class node (tonically active), NOT a driven one at baseline 0.05. Our vlPAG baseline is SCAFFOLD 0.05/0.1 (contrast the grounded pacemakers dPAG-GABA 0.19, LC 0.15). TWO OUTCOMES: (i) vlPAG-glut IS tonic -> Q2 is a GROUNDED BASELINE (the gate taxonomy tells how: paired deviant from the intrinsic rate), and THERE IS NO MISSING DRIVER; (ii) it is driven -> Q2 needs a grounded afferent (the selector completion + the opioid release + mPFC, below). This is the primary Q2 question and it decides whether the fix is a number or an edge. Do NOT add a driver until it is answered. ▲ ANSWERED (RULED): NOT tonic -- Q2 is an EDGE, not a baseline. Stempel & Evans ran the exact test in the dPAG: VGluT2+ neurons NOT spontaneously active (92.8% below 0.04 Hz; mean 0.11 Hz) AND UNCHANGED after picrotoxin block of inhibition (0.039 Hz, P=0.85). Remove the gate entirely and the glut neurons still do not fire -- 'disinhibition only permits; something must drive' CONFIRMED BY EXPERIMENT. Vaughan & Christie's 'leading to activation' is a circuit description (output rises when the brake comes off a DRIVEN population), not self-start -- an unmeasured phrase does not outweigh a measured null. KEEP vlPAG's 0.05/0.1 scaffold; find the DRIVER. ⚠️ EXTRAPOLATION DISCLOSED: the recording is dPAG, not vlPAG -- same lab/technique/exact experiment, but across columns (burned twice on that). Status: ANSWERED BY ANALOGY (dPAG); vlPAG-specific measurement UNRESOLVED -- a vlPAG-contradiction would be a revision, not a surprise.

S49 -- (b) vlPAG-GABA IS THE SELECTOR, and it is INCOMPLETE -- the LC/PGi partial-completion pattern, THIRD time (v14, RULED). vlPAG-GABA is NOT a gate: it is the freezing-vs-flight SELECTOR that INTEGRATES MULTIPLE inputs. Ours has TWO (CeA moderate-strong, BNST moderate) -- 'vlPAG-GABA has a release' was true, 'vlPAG-GABA is afferented' was not. Enumerate its cited afferent set (like LC/PGi) and record INCOMPLETE with the remainder named -- its own pass. ★ THE CITED RELEASE WE LACK IS A WHOLE SYSTEM: opioids INHIBIT vlPAG-GABA -> disinhibit vlPAG-glut -> activate the RVM projection (Vaughan & Christie 1997; DENSE mu-opioid receptor expression -- not a minor arm). NO opioid circuit exists, yet FOUR circuits name opioids in their prose (vlPAG, dPAG, NAc-shell, PAG-PANIC 'soothed by contact/opioids/OT') -- a transmitter in the vocabulary with no connectome, the same shape as an innate reinforcer with no channel (S42). SCOPE: an opioid circuit is a SYSTEM, and PAG-PANIC's soothing arm wants the SAME one -- two registers, one circuit (the preBotC shape); do NOT build it as a footnote to Q2. THE (b) DRIVERS, PrH/MePV test applied: PMd (dorsal premammillary) is ABSENT -> a new node, block-tested before building; mPFC top-down EXISTS and is afferented (dmPFC, vmPFC both present + driven) -> dmPFC/vmPFC->vlPAG PASSES the test, buildable. But (i) answer the tonic question first. ▲ THE WORK (RULED step 2): vlPAG-GABA's cited afferent set, enumerated -- ours = 2 (CeA, BNST); recorded INCOMPLETE. THE DRIVER of vlPAG-glut (Q2's edge), triaged: (1) mPFC top-down BUILDABLE -- dmPFC/vmPFC exist+afferented (passes PrH/MePV); GROUND THE SPECIFIC PROJECTION FIRST (which subdivision -- PL/dmPFC drives fear, IL/vmPFC extinction; which TARGET CELL -- a glut PFC source onto vlPAG-GLUT DRIVES freezing (+), onto the vlPAG-GABA SELECTOR SUPPRESSES it: the target cell decides the sign, again). (2) PMd ABSENT -- block-test first. (3) the OPIOID system -- its own pass (4 circuits name opioids, none afferented; shared with PAG-PANIC's soothing arm; two registers one circuit). The driver grounding (which mPFC subdivision/target cell) needs the literature -- surfaced, not guessed. ▲ REMAINDER NAMED (block-tested against the model): the SELECTOR vlPAG-GABA HAS 2 (CeA, BNST -- both GABAergic/disinhibitory); the OUTPUT vlPAG-glut HAS VMH (low-moderate, WEAK -- Q2's under-driven edge) + DRN (5-HT1A, the correct suppressor). CANDIDATE REMAINDER, by block-test: (1) ★ mPFC top-down -- dmPFC & vmPFC BOTH exist+afferented, no edge to vlPAG yet -> PASSES, the buildable DRIVER; grounding owed BEFORE routing = which subdivision (PL/dmPFC fear-expression vs IL/vmPFC) and which TARGET CELL (onto vlPAG-glut DRIVES freezing +, onto the vlPAG-GABA selector SUPPRESSES it -- target cell decides the sign). (2) OPIOID disinhibition (mu-opioid -| vlPAG-GABA) -- its own SYSTEM pass, no opioid circuit exists (S49 head). (3) PMd (flight driver) -- ABSENT, FAILS the block-test (its afferents are the medial-hypothalamic defensive system; building it moves the incompleteness one synapse further -- PrH/MePV). (4) PBN (exists+afferented, nociceptive arm) & SC-Pv (exists+afferented, collicular flight arm) -- viable candidates, lower priority, grounding owed. NOTHING BUILT: the driver needs the mPFC subdivision/target-cell grounding (reviewer's literature); surfaced as a candidate list for authorisation, not written.

S50 -- ★ THE FREEZING OUTPUT GAP -- partial-completion, 4th and SHARPEST instance (v14, BUILT). vlPAG's ONLY prior efferent was NRA (the VOCAL relay): the freezing column had a felt state (vlPAG), a selector (vlPAG-GABA) and a driver (VMH) but NO FREEZING EFFECTOR -- freezing could never happen because vlPAG had nowhere to send it. The effector `Mc` (magnocellular medulla) is named in the SECOND CLAUSE of the very Tovote 2016 sentence already cited on CeA->vlPAG-GABA ('...excitatory outputs to PRE-MOTOR TARGETS IN THE MAGNOCELLULAR NUCLEUS OF THE MEDULLA'); we built the first clause and not the second. Not a hub whose afferent list we never checked -- a single cited sentence whose second clause we did not build, and cleared (Phase A + every vlPAG ruling since). BUILT: Mc (motor_effector TERMINAL -- AdrenalCortex test: its efferent is the spinal motor system, absent, so it is an EFFECTOR the exact class as NuFac/NuAmb-vocal, WHERE FREEZING GETS READ; excluded from throttle/temperament) + vlPAG->Mc (strong/AMPA, twin of vlPAG->NRA). L7's opening move again (emotional motor system unefferented -> so was the freezing one).

S51 -- THE mPFC 'DRIVER' RESOLVED INTO A BRAKE (v14, BUILT as the selector's 3rd afferent). THE FLOOR IS HELD RED SOLELY BY S56 (the dlPFC cortical over-drive spikes DRN to 0.617 under threat). ★ S56 gates TWO expected reds, not one: test_defensive_threat_produces_freezing (floor) AND test_divergence_does_not_robustly_emerge (the earned-negative dissociation grew 0.0486->0.0585, ~0.008 of it the same S56 over-drive; xfailed with resolution=S56). TWO reds, ONE cause, ONE fix -- a clean closeout, not a messy one; grounding S56 is PREDICTED to return both green. THREE EMERGENT FINDINGS (from grounded curves, not coded) -- the branch's payoff: (1) GROSS reappraisal/suppression (fell out of where two routes land); (2) TREMBLAY aggression trajectory (peaks age 2, declines through childhood, restraint by adolescence -- from DRN->pfc_low_early_high_late); (3) ★ BUCKHOLTZ 2010 mesolimbic reward-hypersensitivity in psychopathy's impulsive-antisocial factor (psychopathic seed reads reward_approach from the grounded VTA reward-DA adolescent bump; margin ~0.005) -- the most study-relevant, the substrate independently recovering a replicated clinical neurobiology. CAVEAT (study limitations): the striatal-hypersensitivity account is ONE of two (vs Glenn&Yang/Hosking cortico-striatal dysfunction); the model lands on hypersensitivity -- acknowledge, don't assert settled. READ-OUT BOUNDARY (confirmed in code, for the observer/psychometric spec): the CORE read-out speaks DOMAINS (reward_approach/executive/...); the STUDY layer speaks OUTCOMES (sophropathic/psychopathic/intermediate); the mapping lives in the study layer and STAYS there -- the core does not know what a psychopath is. classification is the bare dominant domain; the knife-edge is exposed by the margin FIELD (captured in the golden). TWO DOCUMENTED EMERGENT ARTIFACTS (grounded state revealing a knife-edge the scaffold sat on the other side of): the neutral-aggression floor (0.004, DRN's tonic 5-HT network modulation; provocation-specific at ~60x) and the full-throttle punishment ~0 (a retired tonic-NA artifact; +2e-05->-2.8e-04, reframed to non-inversion). NEXT (the climb): D4 (imitation) -> D3 (representation, the purpose). All remaining substrate work -> substrate_hardening_backlog.

circuits.name: Lateral amygdala
circuits.function: Convergence zone; principal associative fear-learning site

circuits.name: Basal amygdala
circuits.function: LA->CeA relay; context/regulation interface

circuits.name: Central amygdala, lateral division (CeL)
circuits.function: The CeA AFFERENT/SELECTOR division. Lump #13 Stage A: the single CeA lump (documented in its own former nodes field as "central nucleus (CeL/CeM)") is split into CEl and CEm. CEl receives ALL 17 external afferents -- sensory, cortical, thalamic, intra-amygdalar and neuromodulatory -- exactly as the anatomy specifies, and drives CEm only via the CEl-GABA feed-forward inhibition. It houses the SOM+/PKC-delta+ mutually-inhibitory selector microcircuit, which is NOT YET BUILT (Stage C) -- so at Stage A CEl carries its full afferent load with NO local recurrent brake. GABAergic.
circuits.nodes: central nucleus, lateral division (CeL); SOM+ and PKC-delta+ populations
circuits.sources: Ciocchi et al. 2010; Haubensak et al. 2010; Fadok et al. 2017

circuits.name: Intercalated cell masses
circuits.function: Inhibitory gate on CeA; substrate of extinction STRUCTURAL ELEMENT -- NOT A REACTIVITY DIAL (v14, ruled: the whole gate class). The temperament throttle is a MODEL CLAIM about what varies between individuals, and a local inhibitory GATE is not a thing an individual is "less of": throttling it DISINHIBITS its target, so a "less reactive" dial yields MORE output -- directionally perverse. The alpha2 argument, verbatim: a structural element is not a reactivity dial any more than it is a learned association. Excluded from seed_substrate ONLY -- it stays LESIONABLE by the scan, which is an experiment, not a claim. The consequential one: ITC is the GABAergic fear-EXTINCTION gate AND the cell THE ENTIRE PREFRONTAL CONTROL ROUTE RUNS THROUGH (vmPFC->ITC, vlPFC->ITC, then ITC->CeA) -- those edges exist precisely because glutamatergic PFC cannot inhibit the amygdala directly. A throttled ITC therefore gives a low-threat temperament a DISINHIBITED AMYGDALA *and* a DISABLED EXTINCTION GATE = impaired prefrontal control over fear, BY CONSTRUCTION. "Low-threat agents show impaired extinction" is exactly the kind of finding the CU study would report, and it would be an artifact of the dial.
circuits.sources: Pare et al. 2004; Likhtik et al. 2008

circuits.name: Bed nucleus of the stria terminalis
circuits.function: Sustained/uncertain threat (anxiety)

circuits.name: Periaqueductal grey, ventrolateral column
circuits.function: PASSIVE-coping defensive output: freezing / quiescence / hyporeactivity. v14 Phase A: split out of the lumped `PAG`, whose own function field read "Defensive output: freeze (vlPAG) / flight (dPAG)" and whose nodes field read "dorsal PAG / ventrolateral PAG" -- two FUNCTIONALLY OPPOSITE columns carried as one node (the amygdala-subnuclei rule, self-declared). vlPAG and dPAG oppose: vlPAG = passive coping, dPAG = active coping.
circuits.sources: Bandler & Shipley 1994; Tovote et al. 2016 (l/vlPAG -> freezing)

circuits.name: Periaqueductal grey, dorsal/dorsolateral column
circuits.function: ACTIVE-coping defensive output: flight / escape / fight motor output. v14 Phase A: split out of the lumped `PAG` (see vlPAG). Opposes vlPAG: active vs passive coping.
circuits.sources: Bandler & Shipley 1994; Tovote et al. 2016 (dlPAG activation -> flight); Wang et al. 2015; Falkner & Lin 2014

circuits.name: dPAG tonically-active GABAergic neurons
circuits.function: The tonically-active local GABAergic gate on dPAG output -- THE ESCAPE THRESHOLD. VGAT+ dPAG neurons fire action potentials TONICALLY IN THE ABSENCE OF SYNAPTIC INPUT (autonomous, like the LC pacemaker) and are the MAJOR SOURCE of inhibition to the VGluT2+ (glutamatergic, output) dPAG neurons; their activity SETS THE THRESHOLD for escape initiation (it dips at escape onset, rises during escape, peaks at termination). v14 Expression Phase A+: ADDED as the MISSING INHIBITORY ELEMENT (principle 1). The v9 neutral floor had been held by CeA->PAG(-) tonically suppressing a LUMPED node -- but CeA's own citation (Tovote 2016) puts it on the FREEZING column, and CeA does not project to dPAG at all. The floor was resting on the lumping; this is what actually restrains dPAG at rest. BASELINE GROUNDED IN THE ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY, NOT in what restores the floor: baseline_activation is the no-synaptic-input relaxation target, and the intrinsic rate measured UNDER SYNAPTIC BLOCKADE is 6.2 +/- 0.84 Hz; against the dPAG strong-drive reference (silicon-probe high-firing population, mean 32.2 +/- 7.0 Hz) that is 6.2/32.2 ~= 0.19. Its homeostatic_setpoint is PAIRED to that baseline (principle 10: baseline_activation and homeostatic_setpoint are ONE quantity -- a circuit's intrinsic target firing rate -- and are set together from the same citation; the generic 0.05/0.1 default pair breaks the moment one half is grounded). The relational fact holds: the GABAergic rate is far above the excitatory population, which is essentially SILENT at rest (VGluT2+ 0.11 +/- 0.068 Hz; 92.8% below 0.04 Hz). PROVISIONAL, like every baseline. ARCHITECTURE: the same source states the general rule -- "Disinhibition of the PAG has been suggested as a mechanism for initiating other instinctive behaviours, such as vocalisations and freezing, with inhibitory long-range projections from forebrain regions inhibiting local GABAergic circuits within the PAG" -- which is CeA->vlPAG-GABA arrived at independently. One architecture, every column: a tonically-active local GABA gate, disinhibited by long-range forebrain inhibitory projections. STRUCTURAL ELEMENT -- NOT A MANIPULATION SURFACE (v14 Phase B step 0, ruled): excluded from every throttle surface (temperament seeding AND scan search). This is the alpha2 ruling one level up -- we pinned the alpha2 autoreceptor against PLASTICITY because a structural regulatory element is not a learned association; the same argument applies verbatim against THROTTLING. An autonomous pacemaker whose rate is grounded electrophysiology is not a reactivity dial, and throttling it is DIRECTIONALLY PERVERSE: threat-down -> gate-down -> dPAG DISINHIBITED -> MORE escape, i.e. a low-threat temperament would produce a WEAKER escape brake. Enforced by a derived PROPERTY (structural_element), never a curated id list.
circuits.sources: Stempel & Evans et al. 2024 Curr Biol (DOI 10.1016/j.cub.2024.05.068) -- "Tonically active GABAergic neurons in the dorsal periaqueductal gray control instinctive escape in mice"

circuits.name: vlPAG GABAergic interneurons
circuits.function: vlPAG GABAergic interneuron population: it INHIBITS the vlPAG glutamatergic output, and the GABAergic long-projections from CeA (Tovote 2016) and BNST (Hao 2019) INHIBIT IT -- so those inputs DISINHIBIT vlPAG output. This is the target cell the CeA->PAG edge ALREADY CITED (Tovote et al. 2016) and did not implement: it resolves the standing tension that CeA->PAG is inhibitory AS A SYNAPSE (CeA is GABAergic) while the amygdala is among PAG's EXCITATORY controls AS A NET EFFECT ON OUTPUT. Both are true; they only contradicted while the target cell was unrepresented. Same motif as CeA-GABA (v14 Phase 1.1) and DRN-GABA (v13) -- the third instance; the recurring failure mode is LUMPING, not wrong signs. STRUCTURAL ELEMENT -- NOT A REACTIVITY DIAL (v14, ruled: the whole gate class). The temperament throttle is a MODEL CLAIM about what varies between individuals, and a local inhibitory GATE is not a thing an individual is "less of": throttling it DISINHIBITS its target, so a "less reactive" dial yields MORE output -- directionally perverse. The alpha2 argument, verbatim: a structural element is not a reactivity dial any more than it is a learned association. Excluded from seed_substrate ONLY -- it stays LESIONABLE by the scan, which is an experiment, not a claim. Throttling it DISINHIBITS the vlPAG freezing column (and it is the CeA/BNST target cell).
circuits.sources: Tovote et al. 2016 Nature 534:206 (CeA GABAergic -> vlPAG GABAergic interneurons -> disinhibits output -> freezing); Hao et al. 2019 Cell Rep 28:616 (BNST/LH GABAergic -> anterior vlPAG GABAergic cells)

circuits.name: Dorsomedial/medial hypothalamus
circuits.function: Autonomic/endocrine defensive drive

circuits.name: Locus coeruleus
circuits.function: Noradrenaline arousal broadcast; the plasticity gate (rule R5) | v14: baseline 0.05->0.15, grounded in LC PACEMAKER ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY (NOT a stability-window fit): LC neurons are autonomous pacemakers firing tonically ~1-3 Hz in quiet waking (central ~2 Hz), against a functional-max ~10-15 Hz under strong salient/stress drive (what activation=1.0 represents) -> tonic/max ~2/13 ~= 0.15 (range ~0.10-0.20 given normalization uncertainty). Cited: Aston-Jones & Cohen 2005 (LC tonic/phasic modes); Berridge & Waterhouse 2003. The generic 0.05 default (a near-silent phasic-relay assumption) was wrong for a tonic pacemaker and was being propped by the (now phasic) CeA->LC drive. This is what supplies LC's tonic noradrenergic tone; phasic CRF bursts (CeA->LC) ride on top; alpha2 autoregulates. PROVISIONAL: baseline set as faithfully as possible now, flagged for the complete-system retune. | v14 (paired-value fix): homeostatic_setpoint 0.1->0.15 to MATCH the pacemaker baseline. A homeostatic setpoint is a neuron's TARGET FIRING RATE; for an autonomous pacemaker that target IS the pacemaker rate, so baseline_activation and homeostatic_setpoint describe the SAME quantity and are set together. At setpoint 0.1 < baseline 0.15 the R4 homeostat fought the pacemaker and eroded CeA->LC over a life-course WITH EVENTS (LC fires -> mean_activity rises above the 0.1 setpoint -> homeo scales LC's incoming weights down): -7.4%/60k; at setpoint 0.15 CeA->LC is stable. Same electrophysiology citation as the baseline (Aston-Jones & Cohen 2005). PROVISIONAL.

circuits.function: Reward-prediction-error teaching signal; the approach plasticity gate (rule R5) | v14 S57 (RULED): baseline_activation=0.18 is the ADOLESCENT-PEAK reference, NOT the adult value -- the ONE case (the adolescent Gaussian family) where the field and the adult value DIVERGE. maturation() peaks at 1.0 @ age 16, so effective peak = 0.18*1.0 = 0.18 @16y; the adult value is the SETTLE = 0.18*floor(0.7) = ~0.136 @25y. For the monotonic families the field IS the adult value; for this bump family it is the peak. Do not 're-correct' 0.18 to 0.136 -- that would collapse the adolescent overshoot (the reward-seeking substrate).

DOMAIN FLAG (v14 Phase A): carried in `interoception_autonomic` because that is where the effector limb lives (IML/SympOut/DMV/NuAmb) and Phase A adds NO NEW DOMAIN -- but this is a BRANCHIOMOTOR nucleus, not autonomic. The model has no motor/effector domain. circuits.function: Serotonergic tone; the principal aggression/impulsivity REGULATOR (2.1b). Signed per target receptor: dampens the attack area (5-HT1A) and facilitates PFC control (5-HT2A). Placed in defensive_threat (its regulatory home; LC-precedent neuromodulator placement). | v14 (RULED): baseline_activation held at 0.05 and marked UNGROUNDED -- PENDING THE DEVELOPMENTAL-BASELINE MECHANISM (S57), NOT scaffold. DRN is a pacemaker whose ADULT tonic rate normalises to ~0.25-0.40 (1.5-2 Hz clock-like / slow-5-HT max ~5-6 Hz; LC/dPAG-GABA precedent), but applying that as a CONSTANT is a category error: it gives a two-year-old an adult 5-HT brake and breaks the aggression keystone's young->aggress half (via DRN->VMHvl 5-HT1A). 0.05-as-a-constant and 0.30-as-a-constant are BOTH wrong; 0.05 is wrong in the direction that does not fabricate an adult brake on a child. The scaffold-low value was accidentally standing in for the serotonergic system's developmental immaturity. Held at 0.05 until the developmental-baseline mechanism exists. setpoint unchanged (0.1). | v14 S57 BASELINE-SEMANTICS (record correctly -- counterintuitive): when DRN's baseline is grounded (S57 step 2), it is the FUNCTIONAL INHIBITORY EFFICACY of the 5-HT system on the aggression circuit -- which matures UP across childhood -- NOT tissue 5-HT (which PEAKS in the first two postnatal years then DECLINES to adult; Hohmann 1988), and NOT raw firing (immature hyperexcitability is a separate cellular feature). It matures up because the RECEPTOR-AND-CIRCUIT machinery matures up: 5-HT1A autoreceptor response + 5-HT innervation of frontal cortex (top-down inhibitory control) develop late while transmitter level falls. -> DRN maps to pfc_low_early_high_late (the frontal inhibitory-control curve), a GROUNDED identification (Booij/Tremblay 2010 locate the aggression-relevant 5-HT deficit in orbitofrontal synthesis = reduced top-down control).
circuits.sources: Nelson & Trainor 2007 Nat Rev Neurosci; Coccaro et al. 2021 Biol Psychiatry; Miczek et al. 2007

The temperament throttle is a MODEL CLAIM about what varies between individuals, and a local inhibitory GATE is not a thing an individual is "less of": throttling it DISINHIBITS its target, so a "less reactive" dial yields MORE output -- directionally perverse. The alpha2 argument, verbatim: a structural element is not a reactivity dial any more than it is a learned association. Excluded from seed_substrate ONLY -- it stays LESIONABLE by the scan, which is an experiment, not a claim. Throttling it DISINHIBITS the DRN 5-HT node.
