circuits.function: Local cortical GABAergic (PV/SST) interneuron for OFC: feedback inhibition holding the pyramidal circuit at an E/I set-point. S56 STAGE 3 -- the FOURTH and last cortical gate, completing the family (dlPFC/vmPFC/dACC already had one; OFC uniquely had ZERO inhibitory afferents). WHY IT WAS THE DEFECT: OFC sat in a mutual POSITIVE-feedback loop with DRN (DRN->OFC 5-HT2A +1 and OFC->DRN AMPA +1, BOTH correctly signed and grounded) with NO braking element anywhere in the loop -- the textbook missing-inhibitory-element signature. An ungated OFC fed the DRN over-drive that suppressed BOTH output columns (DRN -| vlPAG freezing; DRN -| dPAG and DRN -> dPAG-GABA aggression). The fix is to ADD the cited missing element, never to rebalance a correctly-signed edge. Comes online WITH OFC (3.0). STRUCTURAL ELEMENT, not a reactivity dial -- like its three siblings. PLASTIC and homeostatically maintained like every other edge (the S56 clamp experiment was refuted: the plasticity IS the maintenance). | ★ structural_element ADDED (audit finding, ruled): OFC-GABA shipped WITHOUT this flag while all 10 sibling gates carry it. It sits in domain reward_approach, which the temperament map drives from SEEKING -- so a low-SEEKING seed throttled the OFC BRAKE and thereby DISINHIBITED OFC. That is the exact directional perversity the gate class was ruled closed against: a local inhibitory gate is not something an individual is "less of", and throttling one yields MORE output, not less. The guard that should have caught this derived its gate list FROM the flag it was validating, so it passed vacuously.
circuits.source: S56 Stage 3 -- cortical feedback inhibition (PV/SST), the same grounded element as the dlPFC/vmPFC/dACC interneurons.

circuits.function: Local cortical GABAergic (PV/SST) interneuron for dlPFC: feedback inhibition holding the pyramidal circuit at an E/I set-point (prevents runaway excitation). Comes online WITH dlPFC (adult set-point; the adolescent E/I-maturation trajectory is a deliberate future pass, NOT wired here). STRUCTURAL ELEMENT -- NOT A REACTIVITY DIAL (v14, ruled: the whole gate class). The temperament throttle is a MODEL CLAIM about what varies between individuals, and a local inhibitory GATE is not a thing an individual is "less of": throttling it DISINHIBITS its target, so a "less reactive" dial yields MORE output -- directionally perverse. The alpha2 argument, verbatim: a structural element is not a reactivity dial any more than it is a learned association. Excluded from seed_substrate ONLY -- it stays LESIONABLE by the scan, which is an experiment, not a claim. Throttling it DISINHIBITS dlPFC (cortical E/I feedback).
circuits.nodes: dlPFC local GABAergic interneurons (PV basket / SST Martinotti)
circuits.sources: Ferguson & Gao 2018 Front Neural Circuits 12:37 (PV/E-I in PFC); Isaacson & Scanziani 2011 Neuron 72:231; Markram et al. 2004 Nat Rev Neurosci 5:793

circuits.name: Nucleus retroambiguus
circuits.function: The PAG's PREMOTOR RELAY to the vocal/expiratory motoneurons -- the missing link. THE PAG DOES NOT PROJECT DIRECTLY TO NUCLEUS AMBIGUUS: "the PAG uses the NRA as a relay to excite the vocalization muscle motoneurons" (Boers/Holstege 2002), and "the NRA projects directly to the laryngeal and pharyngeal motoneurons in the nucleus ambiguus" (Holstege 1989). NECESSARY BY LESION: bilateral kainic acid into NRA ABOLISHED PAG-induced vocalisation. Contains the vocalisation-specific laryngeal premotor population (RAmVOC), necessary and sufficient for vocal-cord closure, AND predominantly expiration-related neurons driving the intercostal/abdominal motoneurons -- the expiratory half of faciorespiratory coordination. AFFERENT SET IS CLOSED ABOVE THE PONS: the NRA "receives strong projections from the lateral, ventrolateral, and to a lesser extent the dorsomedial part of the caudal midbrain PAG (Holstege 1989), BUT NOT FROM ANY OTHER SUPRAPONTINE BRAIN STRUCTURE (Holstege 1991)" -- so PAG is its only forebrain input, and NO CORTICAL ROUTE REACHES IT (the volitional pathway bypasses PAG and NRA entirely and lands on the motoneuron pools direct -- Phase D wires there, not here). NOT a structural_element: a DRIVEN premotor relay, not a tonic gate. DISCLOSED -- NRA does NOT carry inspiration: "PAG-induced inspiratory excitation remained even after the injections... an additional pathway from the PAG to respiratory motoneurons other than through the NRA is important for mediating PAG-induced inspiratory activation." That inspiratory route is UNMODELLED, and preBotC (which inhibits RAmVOC so inspiration overrides vocal-cord closure) is absent -- so VOCALISATION IS CURRENTLY UN-BREATH-GATED (fidelity, not floor: NRA is driven-only, nothing leaks). FORWARD (noted, NOT built): NRA is a COMBINATORIAL pool -- the PAG selects which premotor population fires, giving vocalisation, vomiting, coughing, sneezing, MATING POSTURE or childbirth. So v14 Phase 5's reproduction wiring already has its premotor node here, and VMH->vlPAG (the lordosis edge routed in Phase A) is the other half of that circuit. DOMAIN `motor_effector` = THE MOTOR LIMB -- PREMOTOR pools AND motoneuron pools, not merely "terminal effectors" (v14 Phase B, ruled). The domain exists so the DISPLAY IS NOT REACHABLE BY A TEMPERAMENT DIAL; a premotor node UPSTREAM of a motoneuron pool is dial-reachable-through, so leaving it in a throttleable domain would collapse the display's construct validity one synapse earlier -- the same failure, further back. Excluded from _THROTTLEABLE_DOMAINS and unmapped by _TEMPERAMENT_DOMAIN.
circuits.sources: Holstege 1989 J Comp Neurol; Holstege 1991; VanderHorst & Holstege 2000 J Comp Neurol 424:251; Boers & Holstege 2002; Subramanian & Holstege; Science 2024 (RAmVOC)

circuits.name: Nucleus ambiguus, compact/semicompact formation
circuits.function: LARYNGEAL/PHARYNGEAL branchiomotor outflow -- the common final effector for vocalisation, used by BOTH the involuntary/emotional and the voluntary pathways. v14 Phase A: ADDED (rename-and-add, not a split). The existing `NuAmb` was correctly built but MIS-SCOPED: its function, its citation (Saper & Stornetta 2015) and both its afferents (PVN, NTS) are purely CARDIAC -- it is the loose/external formation wearing the whole nucleus's name. There was nothing vocal inside it to divide. The vocal formation had NO existence in the model. DOMAIN (v14 Phase B step 0, ruled): `motor_effector` -- the EIGHTH domain, added because branchiomotor nuclei have no honest home in the other seven and, critically, because DOMAIN IS A THROTTLE SURFACE. scan.throttleable_circuits() is DERIVED from domain tags and AUTO-EXTENDS, so Phase A silently enrolled this node into the throttleable set via `interoception_autonomic` -- which would have made "low-interoception agents show less facial expression" TRUE BY CONSTRUCTION (the same construct-validity bug as AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY, one domain over). It would also have collapsed Phase C's justification: the display reads effectors precisely BECAUSE the throttle does not contain them. `motor_effector` is DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from _THROTTLEABLE_DOMAINS and is not mapped by _TEMPERAMENT_DOMAIN -- an effector is not a reactivity dial. NuAmb-cardiac stays `interoception_autonomic`: it genuinely IS autonomic, and splitting the node is what made that distinction expressible. DOMAIN `motor_effector` = THE MOTOR LIMB -- PREMOTOR pools AND motoneuron pools, not merely "terminal effectors" (v14 Phase B, ruled). The domain exists so the DISPLAY IS NOT REACHABLE BY A TEMPERAMENT DIAL; a premotor node UPSTREAM of a motoneuron pool is dial-reachable-through, so leaving it in a throttleable domain would collapse the display's construct validity one synapse earlier -- the same failure, further back. Excluded from _THROTTLEABLE_DOMAINS and unmapped by _TEMPERAMENT_DOMAIN.
circuits.sources: Holstege 2014; Jurgens 2002 (PAG -> NuAmb vocalisation); Saper & Stornetta 2015 (formations)

circuits.name: Magnocellular nucleus of the medulla
circuits.nodes: magnocellular reticular nucleus of the medulla (Mc / MdV)
circuits.function: THE FREEZING PREMOTOR EFFECTOR -- vlPAG's excitatory output target, the SECOND CLAUSE of the Tovote et al. 2016 sentence already cited on CeA->vlPAG-GABA: '...disinhibition of ventrolateral periaqueductal grey excitatory outputs to PRE-MOTOR TARGETS IN THE MAGNOCELLULAR NUCLEUS OF THE MEDULLA.' We built the first clause (CeA->vlPAG-GABA disinhibition) and not the second; vlPAG's ONLY prior efferent was NRA (the VOCAL relay), so the freezing column had a felt state and a driver but NO EFFECTOR -- freezing could never happen because vlPAG had nowhere to send it (partial-completion recorded as completion, the 4th and sharpest instance -- a single cited sentence whose second clause we did not build). AdrenalCortex TEST -> TERMINAL: Mc's efferent is the spinal motor system, which does not exist in the model, so Mc is an EFFECTOR (the exact class as NuFac / NuAmb-vocal) -- it is WHERE FREEZING GETS READ, not a hub whose own efferents are owed. DOMAIN motor_effector: deliberately EXCLUDED from _THROTTLEABLE_DOMAINS and unmapped by _TEMPERAMENT_DOMAIN (an effector is not a reactivity dial) -- the same discipline as the vocal effectors. This is L7's opening move again: the emotional motor system was unefferented; so was the freezing one.
circuits.sources: Tovote et al. 2016 (Nature; CeA->vlPAG-GABA -> vlPAG-glut -> premotor Mc -> freezing) -- the second clause of the sentence already cited on CeA->vlPAG-GABA.

circuits.name: Facial motor nucleus (VII)
circuits.function: Facial branchiomotor outflow -- the CONVERGENCE POINT of the two descending expression pathways: the involuntary/emotional (extrapyramidal, limbic-driven) and the voluntary/volitional (pyramidal, corticobulbar) routes both terminate here. v14 Phase A: ADDED -- absent from the model entirely. It must exist before either pathway can reach it. Wired in Phases B (involuntary) and D (volitional). DOMAIN (v14 Phase B step 0, ruled): `motor_effector` -- the EIGHTH domain, added because branchiomotor nuclei have no honest home in the other seven and, critically, because DOMAIN IS A THROTTLE SURFACE. scan.throttleable_circuits() is DERIVED from domain tags and AUTO-EXTENDS, so Phase A silently enrolled this node into the throttleable set via `interoception_autonomic` -- which would have made "low-interoception agents show less facial expression" TRUE BY CONSTRUCTION (the same construct-validity bug as AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY, one domain over). It would also have collapsed Phase C's justification: the display reads effectors precisely BECAUSE the throttle does not contain them. `motor_effector` is DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from _THROTTLEABLE_DOMAINS and is not mapped by _TEMPERAMENT_DOMAIN -- an effector is not a reactivity dial. NuAmb-cardiac stays `interoception_autonomic`: it genuinely IS autonomic, and splitting the node is what made that distinction expressible. DOMAIN `motor_effector` = THE MOTOR LIMB -- PREMOTOR pools AND motoneuron pools, not merely "terminal effectors" (v14 Phase B, ruled). The domain exists so the DISPLAY IS NOT REACHABLE BY A TEMPERAMENT DIAL; a premotor node UPSTREAM of a motoneuron pool is dial-reachable-through, so leaving it in a throttleable domain would collapse the display's construct validity one synapse earlier -- the same failure, further back. Excluded from _THROTTLEABLE_DOMAINS and unmapped by _TEMPERAMENT_DOMAIN.

Local cortical GABAergic (PV/SST) interneuron for dACC: FEEDBACK inhibition holding the pyramidal circuit at an E/I set-point (prevents runaway excitation). v14 Expression Phase B+: ADDED as THE MISSING INHIBITORY ELEMENT (principle 1). dACC had THREE excitatory afferents (aIns +, DRN 5-HT2A Gq +, BA AMPA +) and NO local inhibition; it only stayed quiet because those afferents were inert. Grounding BA->dACC (Morecraft, cited) gave it real drive and it SATURATED -- BA=1.000 -> dACC=0.894 (no brake) -> dlPFC=1.000 (ceiling) -- which manufactured a 'divergence emerges' artifact in the executive-development arm (an unbraked node drives the outcome measure into its ceiling; a ceiling looks like a large effect). The fix is NOT lowering the cited BA->dACC edge (that is rebalancing real anatomy to hide missing anatomy -- the DRN-GABA error); it is the absent element. RATE GROUNDED FROM CORTICAL FS/PV ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY, BEFORE looking at what restores the tests -- and the electrophysiology gives a DIFFERENT answer from dPAG-GABA on purpose: neocortical PV+ fast-spiking interneurons are NOT autonomous pacemakers. Under synaptic blockade they are largely SILENT (they lack the persistent-Na/HCN pacemaker conductances that make LC-NA and dPAG-VGAT cells fire tonically without input, the citation dPAG-GABA rests on); their high IN-VIVO rate is synaptically DRIVEN feedback, not an intrinsic tone. So baseline_activation (the no-synaptic-input relaxation target) is ~0 -> the SCAFFOLD default (0.05) stands, and this circuit is CORRECTLY NOT a grounded-setpoint deviant (unlike LC/dPAG-GABA). The grounded content is the RECURRENT E-I LOOP, not a tonic rate: the brake engages IN PROPORTION to dACC's own activity, so it damps exactly the saturation it must and does nothing at rest. This mirrors dlPFC-GABA (Ferguson & Gao 2018, cortical E/I feedback) -- the model's own already-correct cortical interneuron. If the grounded loop does not restore the tests, THAT IS A FINDING (the LC / dPAG-GABA ruling, third application) -- the loop weights are the cited cortical-E/I bands, not tuned to the tests. tau=100 (fast-spiking; dACC pyramidal is 200). Comes online WITH dACC (5.0); the adolescent E/I-maturation trajectory is a deliberate future pass, NOT wired here (same deferral as dlPFC-GABA). STRUCTURAL ELEMENT -- NOT A REACTIVITY DIAL (v14 gate class, ruled): excluded from seed_substrate; it stays LESIONABLE by the scan (an experiment, not a claim). Throttling it DISINHIBITS dACC -- directionally perverse. PROVISIONAL, like every baseline. SYSTEMIC: dACC-GABA is the FIRST landmine of a systemic gap, not the last one closed -- the cortical inhibitory-interneuron layer is 2 of 11 (only dlPFC and vmPFC were braked). See gaps-register S18.
circuits.nodes: dACC/midcingulate local GABAergic interneurons (PV basket / SST Martinotti)
circuits.sources: Ferguson & Gao 2018 Front Neural Circuits 12:37 (PV/E-I in PFC); Isaacson & Scanziani 2011 Neuron 72:231; Markram et al. 2004 Nat Rev Neurosci 5:793

circuits.name: Primary motor cortex, face area (M1-face)
circuits.function: v14 Expression Phase D: THE VOLITIONAL (pyramidal) route to the expression effectors -- the origin of the corticobulbar tract. Drives the POSED expression (the 'pyramidal smile'), by EXCITATION, at the convergence point NuFac where it meets the involuntary/emotional route (BA->dACC/M3->NuFac). Reaches BOTH the face (NuFac) and the larynx/pharynx (NuAmb-vocal), bypassing the PAG and NRA entirely. KEY: it can MASK (pose a different expression over the felt one) but CANNOT hold a neutral face -- M1-face is excitatory, so it cannot reduce NuFac below the emotional drive. That is NOT a limitation, it is the PHENOMENON: micro-expression leakage is the two pathways co-activating and neither winning cleanly (Fridlund: trying to inhibit an amused face raises cheek EMG -- counter-activation, and it fails). A model that could hold a perfect neutral face would be WRONG (it would forbid the leakage that makes a concealed emotion detectable). Domain motor_effector (the motor limb, temperament- and scan-excluded). Comes online school-age on the EXISTING pfc_low_early_high_late schedule -- NO curve authored. LUMP: conflates the distinct human laryngeal motor cortex (LMC) into the face-motor node for M1-face->NuAmb-vocal -- registered sub-grain.

circuits.name: Lateral premotor cortex (PMC-l)
circuits.function: v14 Expression Phase D: THE VOLITIONAL SYSTEM'S HUB -- production AND suppression. (1) PRODUCTION: drives M1-face (premotor->motor), so the executive can pose an expression. (2) SUPPRESSION: the volitional suppressor of emotional VOCALISATION -- lateral premotor cortices 'can suppress laughter or crying' (pathological laughing/crying is this suppressor LOST; Master ref S2.1). It suppresses via PAG-PANIC-GABA (see there). Driven by vlPFC (the regulation goal / response selection, Master S3) -- INDEPENDENTLY of the affective circuits (vlPFC is NOT in AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY), which is the ONLY reason the dual-pathway dissociation is measurable: were the volitional route drawn from the affective network, throttling affect would throttle BOTH pathways and the dissociation would be unmeasurable by construction. Domain motor_effector. Online school-age on the existing pfc schedule -- NO curve authored.

circuits.name: PAG-PANIC local GABAergic interneuron
circuits.function: The local GABAergic gate on PAG-PANIC (the vocalisation/separation-distress column) -- ADDED v14 Expression Phase D as THE INTERNEURON THE VOLITIONAL VOCAL SUPPRESSOR ROUTES THROUGH. This answers the long-registered 'does PAG-PANIC need its own gate?' -- YES, and not by argument but because the RECEPTOR-SIGN CONVENTION would not let the suppressor be written any other way: PMC-l is cortical (glutamatergic), so a direct PMC-l->PAG-PANIC edge comes out EXCITATORY (+), and a suppressor that excites is not a suppressor. The honest route is PMC-l -> PAG-PANIC-GABA (excited) -> PAG-PANIC (inhibited) -- vlPFC->ITC exactly (glutamatergic PFC cannot inhibit the amygdala directly; it routes through the GABAergic intercalated cells). ★ THE GATE CLASS, TWO OPPOSITE FUNCTIONS SET BY THE SOURCE'S TRANSMITTER: CeA (GABAergic) -> vlPAG-GABA disinhibits vlPAG = RELEASE; PMC-l (glutamatergic) -> PAG-PANIC-GABA inhibits PAG-PANIC = SUPPRESSION. One local-interneuron architecture; who reaches it decides. SCAFFOLD baseline (recurrent-E-I class, no cited pacemaker rate -- the vlPAG-GABA precedent, NOT dPAG-GABA). STRUCTURAL ELEMENT -- temperament-excluded, scan-lesionable. DEVELOPMENTAL STORY (emergent, not coded): the gate is present early but its cortical suppressor drive (PMC-l) arrives school-age, so the newborn's crying is weakly gated by volition and school-age control emerges -- Thompson 1991 / Master S5, fallen out of the maturation schedule.
circuits.sources: Tovote et al. 2016 Nature 534:206 (CeA GABAergic -> vlPAG GABAergic interneurons -> disinhibits output -> freezing); Hao et al. 2019 Cell Rep 28:616 (BNST/LH GABAergic -> anterior vlPAG GABAergic cells)

circuits.name: Nucleus paragigantocellularis (PGi)
circuits.function: THE MAJOR EXCITATORY (glutamatergic) AFFERENT TO LC -- v14 D6 fix #2 door 1 (RULED). ~73% of LC neurons are synaptically activated by low-intensity single-pulse PGi stimulation (Aston-Jones, Ennis, Pieribone, Nickell & Shipley 1986, Science: 'restricted afferent control of a broad efferent network' -- LC's major inputs are PGi and PrH, both rostral medulla). PGi INTEGRATES exteroceptive + interoceptive input (pain, cardiovascular, respiratory, arousal) and relays it to LC. WHY IT WAS BUILT: CeA was LC's SOLE external afferent (band moderate, a MINOR DENDRITIC/shell input per Van Bockstaele), carrying 100% of the teaching signal ONLY because the majors were absent -- so throttling AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY (which contains CeA) collapsed LC 54% rest/72% threat/73% distress (the D6 #2 ENTAILMENT, principle 1: an artifact of an incomplete hub). With PGi carrying the majority of LC's drive, CeA is MINOR BY ANATOMY -- the entailment dissolves without editing the set or re-banding CeA->LC. RESOLVES TWO REGISTERED ITEMS: PBN->LC and NTS->LC were never 'dormant', they were WRONG-TARGET -- Aston-Jones showed PBN/NTS do NOT project to LC (they terminate ADJACENT); PGi is their integrator. So PBN->PGi, NTS->PGi, nociception->PGi -> PGi->LC. PGi's afferents are AVAILABLE NOW (that is the justification for building it -- it creates no new unafferented hub). Domain interoception_autonomic (NOT temperament-reachable -- if it were defensive_threat the THREAT dial would reach LC through it and widen door 3). This closes DOOR 1 ONLY; #2 stays open (door 3 = the dial scales LC's node; door 2 = the scan).

circuits.name: VMHvl shell GABAergic interneuron
circuits.nodes: ventrolateral VMH shell (GABAergic cells inhibiting the glutamatergic core)
circuits.function: The GABAergic SHELL of VMHvl, inhibiting its glutamatergic CORE -- v14 defensive-drive (A) ADDED. VMH has a cell-poor shell of GABAergic cells that inhibit the core projection neurons; the aggression route from MeA is MePD (GABAergic) -> this shell -> DISINHIBITS the core, the SAME disinhibition architecture as CeA -> vlPAG-GABA (a forebrain projection made net-excitatory-onto-output through the column's own gate). Replaces the old MeA->VMHvl AMPA-DIRECT edge, which gave the right OUTCOME by the wrong MECHANISM (the v9 keystone-flip shape on a keystone edge). RECURRENT-E-I CLASS: scaffold baseline (no cited tonic rate for the VMH shell; ground the loop, not a rate -- the gate taxonomy default; forcing a pacemaker rate would invent one). STRUCTURAL ELEMENT (temperament-excluded, scan-lesionable). Bands inherited byte-for-byte from the twin vlPAG-GABA. Provisional, like every baseline.
circuits.sources: Tovote et al. 2016 Nature 534:206 (CeA GABAergic -> vlPAG GABAergic interneurons -> disinhibits output -> freezing); Hao et al. 2019 Cell Rep 28:616 (BNST/LH GABAergic -> anterior vlPAG GABAergic cells)

circuits.function: LUMP #13 selector build. The SOM+ (CeL-on) arm of the CeL winner-take-all selection microcircuit. Replaces half of the CEl-GABA lump, which modelled BOTH CeL populations as ONE node delivering a BLANKET brake to CeM. SOM+ and PKC-delta+ are MUTUALLY INHIBITORY, so only one is active at a time and CeL inhibition of CeM is ROUTED, never blanket (Fadok et al. 2017, "a competitive inhibitory circuit for selection of active and passive fear responses"). SOM+ selects the PASSIVE/freezing mode: it inhibits PKC-delta+ (releasing the freezing output) and inhibits the active-coping output. Driven preferentially by the LA conditioned-threat pathway (Li et al. 2013 -- LA->CeL SOM+, potentiated by fear conditioning). STRUCTURAL ELEMENT, not a reactivity dial. | CITATIONS CORRECTED (independent fabrication audit, ruled). The mutual inhibition is REAL but Fadok 2017 was the wrong citation for THIS pair: Fadok's competing populations are CRF+ vs SOM+ (it never uses "SOM-negative" and never mentions PKC-delta). The SOM+/PKC-delta+ reciprocal inhibition is grounded instead by HAUBENSAK et al. 2010 ("PKC-delta+ neurons make reciprocal inhibitory synapses with PKC-delta- neurons in CEl") and LI et al. 2013 ("SOM+ and SOM- neurons in CeL mutually inhibit"). Fadok 2017 REMAINS correctly cited for the FREEZING half (SOM+ activation initiates passive freezing) and for the competitive active/passive selection ARCHITECTURE. APPROXIMATION NOW STATED RATHER THAN ASSUMED: SOM- is treated as PKC-delta+, which Li 2013 quantifies as LARGELY but not wholly non-overlapping (only 13 +/- 1% of SOM+ neurons express PKC-delta; the two together cover ~80% of CeL, so ~20% is neither). McCullough 2018 further reports Crf/Sst CO-LOCALISATION in CeL, so the flight-arm identity is an approximation, not an identity.
circuits.nodes: central amygdala lateral division (CeL) GABAergic neurons
circuits.sources: Knobloch et al. 2012 Neuron 73:553 (OT->CeL->CeM microcircuit gating fear output)
circuits.name: CeL SOM+ population (CeL-on)

circuits.function: LUMP #13 selector build. The PKC-delta+ (CeL-off) arm of the CeL winner-take-all selection microcircuit. Selects the ACTIVE-coping mode: it inhibits the CeM freezing output (the classic CeL->CeM feed-forward inhibition, Ciocchi/Haubensak 2010) while being itself suppressed when SOM+ wins. Driven preferentially by the PBN CGRP nociceptive pathway (Han et al. 2015 -- CGRP PBN->CeL PKC-delta+) and by oxytocin (Knobloch et al. 2012: OT excites CeL and thereby REDUCES CeM output/freezing -- placing OT here is what preserves that measured net effect). STRUCTURAL ELEMENT, not a reactivity dial. | CITATIONS CORRECTED (independent fabrication audit, ruled). The mutual inhibition is REAL but Fadok 2017 was the wrong citation for THIS pair: Fadok's competing populations are CRF+ vs SOM+ (it never uses "SOM-negative" and never mentions PKC-delta). The SOM+/PKC-delta+ reciprocal inhibition is grounded instead by HAUBENSAK et al. 2010 ("PKC-delta+ neurons make reciprocal inhibitory synapses with PKC-delta- neurons in CEl") and LI et al. 2013 ("SOM+ and SOM- neurons in CeL mutually inhibit"). Fadok 2017 REMAINS correctly cited for the FREEZING half (SOM+ activation initiates passive freezing) and for the competitive active/passive selection ARCHITECTURE. APPROXIMATION NOW STATED RATHER THAN ASSUMED: SOM- is treated as PKC-delta+, which Li 2013 quantifies as LARGELY but not wholly non-overlapping (only 13 +/- 1% of SOM+ neurons express PKC-delta; the two together cover ~80% of CeL, so ~20% is neither). McCullough 2018 further reports Crf/Sst CO-LOCALISATION in CeL, so the flight-arm identity is an approximation, not an identity.

circuits.name: CeL PKC-delta+ population (CeL-off)
CeM passive-defence output population
circuits.function: LUMP #13 selector build. The CeM output population carrying the PASSIVE/freezing arm (-> vlPAG-GABA, which disinhibits the vlPAG freezing column). Real CeM output neurons are heterogeneous, projecting differentially to PAG vs hypothalamus; modelling CeM as ONE node meant a single activation drove both opposite-signed outputs, so the coupling merely relocated from the CeA lump to CeM. Gated by CEl-PKCd; released when SOM+ wins. Mirrors PsychSim's own v14 Phase A vlPAG (passive coping) / dPAG (active coping) split. | CITATIONS CORRECTED (independent fabrication audit, ruled). The mutual inhibition is REAL but Fadok 2017 was the wrong citation for THIS pair: Fadok's competing populations are CRF+ vs SOM+ (it never uses "SOM-negative" and never mentions PKC-delta). The SOM+/PKC-delta+ reciprocal inhibition is grounded instead by HAUBENSAK et al. 2010 ("PKC-delta+ neurons make reciprocal inhibitory synapses with PKC-delta- neurons in CEl") and LI et al. 2013 ("SOM+ and SOM- neurons in CeL mutually inhibit"). McCullough 2018 further reports Crf/Sst CO-LOCALISATION in CeL, so the flight-arm identity is an approximation, not an identity.
circuits.sources: Ciocchi et al. 2010; Haubensak et al. 2010; Fadok et al. 2017

circuits.name: CeM active-defence output population
circuits.function: LUMP #13 selector build. The CeM output population carrying the ACTIVE-defence arm (-> HYPdm). Gated by CEl-SOM; released when PKC-delta+ wins. Grounded in Fadok et al. 2017 (CeL SOM+ -> freezing / SOM- -> flight) mapped onto PsychSim's existing vlPAG/dPAG passive-vs-active coping split. | CITATIONS CORRECTED (independent fabrication audit, ruled). The mutual inhibition is REAL but Fadok 2017 was the wrong citation for THIS pair: Fadok's competing populations are CRF+ vs SOM+ (it never uses "SOM-negative" and never mentions PKC-delta). Fadok 2017 REMAINS correctly cited for the FREEZING half (SOM+ activation initiates passive freezing) and for the competitive active/passive selection ARCHITECTURE. APPROXIMATION NOW STATED RATHER THAN ASSUMED: SOM- is treated as PKC-delta+, which Li 2013 quantifies as LARGELY but not wholly non-overlapping (only 13 +/- 1% of SOM+ neurons express PKC-delta; the two together cover ~80% of CeL, so ~20% is neither). McCullough 2018 further reports Crf/Sst CO-LOCALISATION in CeL, so the flight-arm identity is an approximation, not an identity.
circuits.sources: Ciocchi et al. 2010; Haubensak et al. 2010; Fadok et al. 2017

circuits.function: LUMP #13 CLOSE -- the missing half of the grounded competing pair. Fadok et al. 2017 ("A competitive inhibitory circuit for selection of active and passive fear responses", Nature 542:96) establishes that CeL selects active vs passive defence via TWO MUTUALLY-INHIBITORY populations: CRF+ mediates conditioned FLIGHT, SOM+ initiates passive FREEZING. The model had only SOM+, and an INFERRED SOM+->CEm-active edge was standing in for this population -- which is why removing that refuted edge REVEALED the gap rather than causing it: the active arm had no population to select it, so it simply followed its BA drive (measured: CEm-active 0.272/0.273/0.274 across affiliation at threat 1.0, i.e. completely insensitive to which population won). THE MODEL HAD CONFLATED TWO DISTINCT AXES: Haubensak 2010's PKC-delta+ is a GATING axis (CeL-off gates CeM output -- retained, correctly grounded); Fadok's CRF+/SOM+ is the SELECTION axis (which defensive mode wins). This is the lumping pattern one level further in, and the same defect that opened Lump #13. GABAergic, like its CeL siblings. REGISTERED SIMPLIFICATION: Fadok reports CRF+/SOM+ as largely non-overlapping, but McCullough 2018 and Hartley 2019 report partial Crf/Sst co-localisation in CeL. The populations are modelled as distinct because THAT is the competitive-selection mechanism; the partial overlap is a fidelity item, not this pass.

retinogeniculostriate / primary visual pathway: general visual input is transduced at the retina (RET) and relayed RET->LGN->V1->V-ventral (cortical ventral stream). Textbook anatomy -- retina->LGN->V1 is the primary visual pathway in every neuroanatomy text (Hubel & Wiesel established V1's geniculate input). ADDED (HSO-shelved restoration, design 2.11): the connectome carried the cortical visual stream (RET->LGN->V1->V-ventral->OFC) PRESENT BUT STARVED -- no visual input reached RET; the visual cue entered only at SC-Pv (subcortical retinotectal, threat/orienting). This edge routes general visual input to the retina so a visual cue can reach the cortex, where the plastic V-ventral->OFC associative site can grow a cue->reward association THROUGH EXPERIENCE. It does NOT encode cue->reward -- it routes sensory input; the association emerges via use-dependent plasticity at the associative site from near-zero. anatomy basis.

connections.source: 'social pain': ACC drives distress on social loss (overlaps physical-pain ACC); contested interpretation; strength assumed || v14 Phase B -- RE-MARKED assumption -> anatomy. The marker had conflated "the SOCIAL-PAIN INTERPRETATION is contested" with "the EDGE is assumed". The EDGE is established backbone: the cingulo-periaqueductal vocalisation pathway -- "one pathway runs from the anterior cingulate cortex via the periaqueductal gray into the reticular formation of pons and medulla, and from there to the phonatory motoneurons"; the ACC "plays a role in voluntary INITIATION of vocal behavior, while the PAG is involved in vocal GATING at a more elementary level"; Jurgens placed the ACC "at the apex of a hierarchical cingulo-periaqueductal pathway", established over decades by tracing, stimulation, lesion and inactivation (Jurgens 1994/2002). Same shape as the v13 DRN re-mark. The SOCIAL-PAIN reading of this edge REMAINS contested and stays flagged; the weight remains SCAFFOLD. What changed is provenance, not the number.

v14 defensive-drive (A): MeA -> VMH SIGN RE-GROUNDED to glutamatergic (+). MePV cells that project to VMHdm (our VMH, the predator-defence node) are GLUTAMATERGIC and fire to CAT ODOR (Choi et al.; the MeA/MePV predator-defence arm). The fallback had picked GABA (-1) purely by transmitter-field TYPING ORDER ('GABA/glutamate' -> GABA; S44), silently suppressing the defensive column and leaving vlPAG dead. This gives VMH -> vlPAG its excitatory drive (necessary-not-sufficient: Q2, the VMH->vlPAG band, re-runs after). ★ LUMP COST DISCLOSED (S45): MeA is the tenth lump (MePV glut/predator vs MePD GABA/reproduction) and cannot be split -- MePV's defining afferent is PREDATOR ODOR, which has no input channel (MeA <- PIR, V-ventral only), so a split would make MePV/MePD undrivable-apart (the PrH block). So this edge is glutamatergic and MeA->VMHvl-GABA is GABAergic (sign is per-EDGE), and both are right -- BUT MePV and MePD still fire TOGETHER (one activation), so a predator cue and a conspecific cue drive BOTH routes. That cost stays until the input surface can tell a predator from a conspecific (S45 predator-odor channel gap).

sexual-behaviour motor output via PAG; animal-dominant | v14 Phase A -> vlPAG: routed BY ITS CITED FUNCTION (the lordosis/sexual-behaviour motor output runs through the vlPAG column). v14 Q1 (RULED): receptor RE-GROUNDED None->AMPA. VMH's long-range PROJECTION cells are the glutamatergic CORE; the GABAergic population is the cell-poor SHELL, which is LOCAL (inhibits the core) and does NOT project -- so a long-range VMH projection cannot be GABAergic. The +1 sign is now CITED (glutamatergic core), not a typing-order fallback (S44). Sign UNCHANGED (+1), band UNTOUCHED (low-moderate). ★ S44's sharpest lesson: MeA->VMH's typing order gave the WRONG sign, VMH->vlPAG's gave the RIGHT one -- same mechanism, opposite outcomes; you cannot tell from the OUTCOME whether the sign is grounded. The census hunts UNGROUNDED signs, of which the wrong ones are a random subset. | v14 (RULED, option b): weight is NOT groundable in isolation -- it is a COLUMN question (principle 6). The literature (Wang, Chen & Lin, Neuron 2015) puts VMHdm/c's immobility drive on the DORSOLATERAL PAG (dlPAG -> inflexible/attentive immobility), NOT the vlPAG (passive/quiescent freeze, the CeA->vlPAG Tovote route). Our edge lands on vlPAG ONLY because the dorsolateral column is FUSED into our dPAG (the dl/l lump, already registered) -- so VMH's immobility drive had no correct target (dPAG=active/flight; vlPAG=passive). This edge STANDS IN FOR VMH->dlPAG pending the dl/l split (its correction condition). GROUNDED VALUE = STRONG, not low-moderate: optogenetic activation drives immobility outright -- a behaviourally-decisive driver, not a weak modulator. APPLIED (D6 BUILD 1): low-moderate->STRONG, basis anatomy->literature (Wang/Chen/Lin 2015). Column caveat stands: stands in for VMH->dlPAG; correction = the dl/l split (backlog). ★ The freezing driver was never weak -- low-moderate under-weighted a decisive immobility drive, part of why freezing wouldn't fire.

connections.source: alpha2-adrenergic AUTORECEPTOR: NA released by LC acts back on somatodendritic alpha2 autoreceptors ON LC (Gi -> inhibitory), the intrinsic negative feedback that limits LC firing (Aghajanian & VanderMaelen 1982; Cedarbaum & Aghajanian). STRUCTURAL / NON-PLASTIC (bounds-pinned): an autoreceptor is a fixed regulatory element, not a learned association, so BCM must not depress it. Value is its GROUNDED pharmacological band (moderate LC autoregulation), NOT chosen to stabilise the loop -- the PHASIC CeA->LC drive prevents the latch, so alpha2 is verified NOT load-bearing (the loop stays stable as alpha2 varies). A self-edge (source==target) representing autoregulation.

IW-ATTRACT-REWARD (catalogue, cited): attractive faces intrinsically rewarding to a perceiver -- Langlois 2000; Aharon 2001 (NAcc; willing-to-work-to-view). SCAFFOLD magnitude; weight sex_conditioned. On IN-CONSPEC (social-visual valuation), NOT IN-VIS.
IW-ATTRACT-REWARD (catalogue, cited): medial-OFC response to facial attractiveness -- O'Doherty 2003 'Beauty in a smile'. SCAFFOLD magnitude; weight sex_conditioned.

IW-FORMIDABILITY-SUBMIT (catalogue, cited): perceived formidability drives the PERCEIVER'S defensive/submission (CeA->PAG), NOT attack (VMHvl) -- Sell et al. 2009 Proc R Soc B. SCAFFOLD magnitude; weight sex_conditioned.

v14 Phase-2 kinship: the self-referent kin-recognition signal (signature_match) drives oxytocin. OT mediates BOTH social recognition (Oettl et al. 2016, Neuron -- OT modulates conspecific-odour recognition) and social reward (Dolen et al. 2013, Nature; Hung et al. 2017, Science -- OT is the social-reinforcement signal in NAc/VTA, signalling the salience of a FAMILIAR partner), so a kin-recognition cue reaching affiliation runs THROUGH oxytocin -> the now-drivable PVN-OT -> the Phase-1 bonding scaffold (PVN-OT->NAc-shell/MeA/SEPT/MPOA). Collapses the chemosignal-detection->OT relay into the sensory drive, mirroring the affective_touch->PVN-OT edge. innate_reinforcer: favouring self-similar kin is an innate primary social bias. NEPOTISM EMERGES from OT driving bonding -- NOT a coded similarity->affiliation coefficient; the cue is self-similarity (signature_match: self-referent, signature-only), relatedness never downstream of spawn.

dev-social integration pass (F2): the RECOGNITION cue of a positively-regarded KNOWN other -- a familiar conspecific recognised as SAFE/warm -- drives oxytocin/approach, mirroring IN-CONSPEC:kin_signature->PVN-OT byte-for-byte (weight+receptor treatment). GROUNDED: dorsal-CA2 hippocampal social-recognition memory carries an abstract social VALENCE (safe vs threat) whose approach arm runs through OT -- Kassraian 2024 Nat Neurosci (dCA2 discriminates social threat from safety); Oettl 2016 Neuron (OT modulates conspecific-odour recognition); Dolen 2013 Nature / Hung 2017 Science (OT signals the salience of a FAMILIAR partner). LUMP (option i, matching the 3 sibling IN-CONSPEC bands' direct grain): the dCA2->OT relay is collapsed into the direct cue->PVN-OT edge, exactly as kin_signature collapses chemosignal-detection->OT; the explicit dCA2/vCA1/lateral-septum recognition node is REGISTERED as a future un-lumping pass. The cue MAGNITUDE is computed from the perceiver's stored relationship (familiarity gates, positive affect signs this arm, trust modulates) -- LEARNED, per-other; 'innate_reinforcer' marks the PATHWAY (the OT social-reward system), not the partner-values. Affiliation to a known-warm other EMERGES from OT driving bonding, never a coded history->affiliation coefficient.

dev-social integration pass (F2): the RECOGNITION cue of a distrusted/hostile KNOWN other -- a familiar conspecific recognised as a THREAT -- drives the PERCEIVER'S defensive system, mirroring IN-CONSPEC:formidability_cue->CeA byte-for-byte (defence, NOT attack/VMHvl). GROUNDED: dCA2 discriminates social threat from safety and its defensive/avoidance arm runs dCA2/vHPC -> lateral septum -> defensive -- Kassraian 2024 Nat Neurosci (silencing dCA2 -> generalised social avoidance; the animal can no longer tell safe from threatening and defends against both). LUMP (option i, matching the sibling IN-CONSPEC grain): dCA2->LS->defensive collapsed into the direct cue->CeA edge; explicit recognition node registered as a future un-lumping pass. The cue MAGNITUDE is computed from the perceiver's stored relationship (familiarity gates, negative affect + low trust drive this arm) -- LEARNED, per-other. Wariness of a known-hostile other EMERGES from the perceiver's defensive circuits, never a coded history->threat coefficient.

The 'assumption' marker had conflated 'scaffold weight' with 'assumed edge' -- the edge is well-grounded backbone. | S56 STAGE 1 (E-I GROUNDING, detailed balance): weight DERIVED from the grounded relation 'inhibitory feedback tracks excitatory drive PROPORTIONALLY' -- w_up = w_reference x (load_node / load_reference), REFERENCE = dACC (3 circuit excitatory afferents, loop left UNCHANGED at moderate=0.5). load_vmPFC = 5 circuit excitatory afferents (HPCv, VTA, dlPFC, AdrenalCortex, DRN). Derivation: 0.5 x 5/3 = 0.8333 (within the [0,1] bound -- unlike dlPFC, vmPFC achieves TRUE proportionality). Set-point inherited from the dACC reference, never fitted to an outcome; the normalized settling EMERGES from proportional inhibition in the divisive integrator, not separately imposed. No free absolute parameter.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: GABA-A INHIBITORY -- PV/SST feedback inhibition holds vmPFC at an E/I set-point off ceiling (Ferguson&Gao 2018; the missing cortical inhibitory partner).

The 'assumption' marker had conflated 'scaffold weight' with 'assumed edge' -- the edge is well-grounded backbone. | S56 STAGE 1 note: this is the INHIBITORY RETURN (gate->node) and is UNCHANGED -- the grounded load-scaling is applied to the EXCITATORY feedback drive (node->gate), because detailed balance puts the load dependence on the interneuron's excitatory input (the population it inhibits drives it), while the GABA-A synaptic strength is a synaptic property, not a load-dependent one.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- OFC pyramidal cells excite their local PV/SST interneuron (the feedback-inhibition drive limb), as in the three sibling cortical loops.
connections.source: S56 STAGE 3 -- the drive limb of OFC's recurrent E-I loop. LOAD-SCALED under Stage 1's grounded relation (detailed balance: inhibitory feedback tracks excitatory drive PROPORTIONALLY): dACC reference load 3 at w 0.50, OFC load 4 -> 0.50 x 4/3 = 0.6667. NO CLAMP NEEDED -- OFC is the LIGHTEST-loaded cortical node, so true proportionality is fully expressible here (unlike dlPFC load 9 -> 1.5 and CeA load 15 -> 2.5, both bound-clamped to 1.0). Left PLASTIC like every other edge: the homeostat MAINTAINS the balance (the clamp experiment was refuted by measurement -- freezing the limbs saturated the cortex at rest).

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: GABA-A INHIBITORY -- the PV/SST interneuron's inhibitory return onto the OFC pyramidal population, holding it at an E/I set-point. Same receptor and band as the dlPFC/vmPFC/dACC returns (moderate-strong): the family's grounded synaptic strength, not a load-dependent quantity.
connections.source: S56 STAGE 3 -- the inhibitory return limb of OFC's recurrent E-I loop, completing the cortical gate family. Mirrors the three sibling returns byte-for-byte in band and receptor (no new knob). This is the braking element the DRN<->OFC positive-feedback loop lacked.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- dlPFC pyramidal cells excite their local interneuron (feedforward/feedback drive).
connections.source: v12c cortical E/I interneuron. AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- dlPFC pyramidal cells excite their local interneuron (feedforward/feedback drive). | RE-MARKED assumption->anatomy (HSO-shelved restoration, design §2.11): structural-regulatory BACKBONE (grounded existence -- DRN serotonergic system / interneuron E/I feedback / Allen afferent), scaffold weight; NOT an associative site. The 'assumption' marker had conflated 'scaffold weight' with 'assumed edge' -- the edge is well-grounded backbone. | S56 STAGE 1 (E-I GROUNDING, detailed balance): weight is no longer the family's one-band scaffold -- it is DERIVED from the grounded relation 'inhibitory feedback tracks excitatory drive PROPORTIONALLY' (detailed balance: stimulus-invariant E/I proportionality per input; the feedback interneuron is driven by the population it inhibits, so its drive scales with that population's afferent load). w_up = w_reference x (load_node / load_reference), where the REFERENCE is dACC (lightest cortical load, 3 circuit excitatory afferents, its loop left UNCHANGED at moderate=0.5) and load_dlPFC = 9 circuit excitatory afferents (MDthal, S2-PPC, dACC, FPC, OFC, HPCv, VTA, LC, BF-ACh). Derivation: 0.5 x 9/3 = 1.5 -> CLAMPED to the weight bound 1.0. ** HONEST LIMITATION: dlPFC hits the [0,1] ceiling, so the heaviest node is UNDER-corrected relative to true proportionality (it gets 2x the reference gain, not the 3x proportionality requires) -- the ceiling, not the relation, is the binding constraint here. ** The set-point is INHERITED from the dACC reference, never fitted to an outcome; the normalized/sublinear settling EMERGES from proportional inhibition in the divisive integrator (a = baseline + sum(inputs) -> a_node ~ E/(1+loop_gain)) and is NOT separately imposed. No free absolute parameter: one node fixed, the others derived from it and the connectome's own afferent counts.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: GABA-A INHIBITORY -- PV/SST feedback inhibition holds dlPFC at an E/I set-point off ceiling (Ferguson&Gao 2018; the missing cortical inhibitory partner).
connections.source: v12c cortical E/I interneuron. GABA-A INHIBITORY -- PV/SST feedback inhibition holds dlPFC at an E/I set-point off ceiling (Ferguson&Gao 2018; the missing cortical inhibitory partner). | RE-MARKED assumption->anatomy (HSO-shelved restoration, design §2.11): structural-regulatory BACKBONE (grounded existence -- DRN serotonergic system / interneuron E/I feedback / Allen afferent), scaffold weight; NOT an associative site. The 'assumption' marker had conflated 'scaffold weight' with 'assumed edge' -- the edge is well-grounded backbone.

The tonically-active VGAT+ dPAG population is the MAJOR SOURCE of synaptic inhibition to the VGluT2+ (glutamatergic, output) dPAG neurons -- this is the escape threshold itself. Local GABA_A antagonism in PAG makes a previously sub-threshold stimulus suprathreshold, i.e. this inhibitory tone is what holds the floor. SCAFFOLD band. Stempel & Evans et al. 2024 Curr Biol (DOI 10.1016/j.cub.2024.05.068) -- "Tonically active GABAergic neurons in the dorsal periaqueductal gray control instinctive escape in mice"

v14: RESOLVES the standing "PAG 5-HT 1A/2A mixed" flag -- the resolution is the TARGET CELL, not the receptor. 5-HT2A agonists inhibit escape BY ACTIVATING THE GABAergic INTERNEURONS LOCATED IN THE dPAG (bicuculline blocks the anti-escape effect, proving the route). So 5-HT2A (Gq, EXCITATORY) onto the GATE -> NET INHIBITION of escape. Paired with DRN->dPAG on 5-HT1A (Gi, inhibitory) direct onto the output -> ALSO inhibition of escape. TWO RECEPTORS, OPPOSITE SIGNS, SAME BEHAVIOURAL OUTCOME -- because they land on DIFFERENT CELLS. The receptor-derived-sign convention is vindicated, not strained. SCAFFOLD band.

The vocal limb, unambiguous arm: the NRA "receives STRONG projections from the LATERAL, VENTROLATERAL... part of the caudal midbrain PAG" and "stimulatory activation of the lateral and ventrolateral PAG generates vocalization" (Holstege 1989; VanderHorst & Holstege 2000). vlPAG IS the ventrolateral column. SCAFFOLD band.

The vocal limb, LATERAL arm -- DISCLOSED LUMP: NRA's input is from the LATERAL column, and the literature is specific that the DORSOLATERAL column produces hyperpneic tachypnea, NOT vocalisation. Our `dPAG` is seeded "dorsal PAG / flight" (Bandler's dl) but carries VMHvl->dPAG for ATTACK, which is the LATERAL column's function -- so dPAG is in fact the dl-l COMPLEX, and this edge is grounded via its LATERAL component. THE MODEL CANNOT CURRENTLY EXPRESS THAT THE DORSOLATERAL HALF DOES NOT PROJECT. Registered: the dl/l dissociation meets the grain test (different afferents, outputs, analgesia, and now a different NRA projection) -- dPAG is the next split, NOT now (it would re-open the aggression keystone). TENSION DISCLOSED: a mouse separation-call literature puts the DORSOLATERAL PAG in the vocal route (dlPAG -> neurotensin+ NRA neurons), against the cat/monkey stimulation result -- species/paradigm difference, recorded not resolved. SCAFFOLD band.

The cry finally OUTPUTS. VERIFIED, NOT INFERRED: "separation-induced low-frequency vocalizations are produced by a neural circuit encompassing neurons in the PAG projecting to neurotensin-positive neurons in nucleus retroambiguus", and "the NRA is a hindbrain premotor region that regulates the production of BOTH ultrasonic vocalizations AND DISTRESS CALLS (squeaks)". So the separation-distress call routes through the same common vocal premotor. Band `moderate` rather than `strong`: PAG-PANIC is our periventricular/ dorsomedial separation-distress column, and Holstege has NRA receiving from the dorsomedial PAG "TO A LESSER EXTENT" than the l/vl. SCAFFOLD band; the column mapping is disclosed, not measured.

"The NRA projects DIRECTLY to the laryngeal and pharyngeal motoneurons in the nucleus ambiguus in the lateral medulla" (Holstege 1989; Boers 2002). NECESSARY BY LESION: bilateral kainic acid into NRA ABOLISHED PAG-induced vocalisation -- the same shape of proof as silencing the dPAG gate. This edge is what makes the emotional motor system EFFERENT for the first time. SCAFFOLD band.

THE EMOTIONAL ROUTE TO THE FACE. The midcingulate contains two premotor FACE areas -- M3 (rostral) and M4 (caudal) (Morecraft et al. 2004). "A candidate pathway with strong anatomical support proceeds from the face-perception system through the AMYGDALA to cingulate area M3, and FROM THERE, DIRECTLY TO THE FACIAL NUCLEUS"; M4 "targets the lateral regions of the facial nucleus". DISCLOSED LUMP (same class as PAG): our `dACC` node is seeded nodes="dorsal ACC / mid-cingulate" but function="Conflict/error monitoring; effort; expected value of control" -- i.e. it LUMPS the COGNITIVE anterior cingulate with the MIDCINGULATE MOTOR areas M3/M4. This edge is grounded via its midcingulate-motor component. Registered: the cingulate motor split, and the MISSING amygdalo-cingulate link -- Morecraft's cited route is BA -> M3 -> NuFac, and BA->dACC does NOT exist in the model (dACC's affective drive currently arrives via aIns->dACC instead). SCAFFOLD band.

THE LIMBIC DRIVE TO THE FACE -- Morecraft's cited afferent, previously MISSING. 'A ROBUST projection exists from the lateral basal nucleus of the amygdala to Layers II, IIIa, and V of the rostral cingulate motor cortex (M3)' (Morecraft et al. 2004; Morecraft 2007 amygdalo-cingulate), completing the cited emotional route: face-perception system -> AMYGDALA -> cingulate M3 -> DIRECTLY TO THE FACIAL NUCLEUS. Band `moderate-strong` reflects the literature's own qualifier ('robust'); SCAFFOLD, and NOT chosen to make the face move -- the facial response is MEASURED after, whatever it gives. WHY IT WAS BLOCKING: before this edge the facial route had NO direct affective drive at all. Traced backward it was NuFac <- dACC <- {aIns, DRN} only, and the amygdala's SHORTEST path to the face ran BA -> pSTS -> rSMG-TPJ -> aIns -> dACC -> NuFac -- i.e. THROUGH pSTS AND rSMG-TPJ, which ARE the COGNITIVE_MENTALIZING set. So the 'emotional' facial route was in fact a COGNITIVE route wearing that name, and it ran through precisely the network the CU manipulation SPARES -- which would have made the facial half of the psychopathy expression signature (reduced mimicry, shallow affect) untestable or backwards. Contrast the vocal half, which was already complete and limbic-driven: CeA -| vlPAG-GABA -| vlPAG -> NRA -> NuAmb-vocal. LUMP DISCLOSED (as for dPAG->NRA): our `dACC` is nodes='dorsal ACC / mid-cingulate' but function='Conflict/error monitoring; effort; expected value of control' -- it conflates the COGNITIVE anterior cingulate with the MIDCINGULATE MOTOR areas M3/M4, so this edge attributes to the whole node what M3 carries. Registered as the third split candidate, queued behind dPAG's dl/l split; neither now.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- dlPFC pyramidal cells excite their local interneuron (feedforward/feedback drive).
connections.source: v14 Expression Phase B+ cortical E/I interneuron. AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- dACC pyramidal cells excite their local interneuron (feedback drive). Mirrors dlPFC->dlPFC-GABA. Band = the cited cortical-E/I 'moderate', NOT tuned to the tests.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- dlPFC pyramidal cells excite their local interneuron (feedforward/feedback drive).
connections.source: v14 Expression Phase B+ cortical E/I interneuron. GABA-A INHIBITORY -- PV/SST feedback inhibition holds dACC at an E/I set-point off ceiling (Ferguson & Gao 2018, the corticolimbic E/I precedent dlPFC-GABA rests on). THE MISSING BRAKE that the BA->dACC drive exposed (principle 1). Band = the cited 'moderate-strong', mirroring dlPFC-GABA->dlPFC, NOT chosen to restore the tests.

v14 Phase D THE POSED FACE: corticobulbar (pyramidal) direct to the facial nucleus (Kuypers; Morecraft). Band = moderate, BALANCED with the emotional route dACC->NuFac (moderate) so the volitional/emotional competition at NuFac emerges from dynamics, NOT a band choice. Excitatory -> it MASKS, cannot silence.

v14 Phase D THE POSED VOICE: corticobulbar to the laryngeal/pharyngeal motoneurons, BYPASSING PAG and NRA (the cortico-bulbar route reaches both effectors). THIS IS THE CROSSTALK THAT TESTS S22: a single volitional source now reaches both the face and the voice, so the modality dissociation (pain->face, sep->voice) is no longer guaranteed by an absence. LUMP: M1-face here stands in for the distinct laryngeal motor cortex (LMC) -- registered. SCAFFOLD band.

v14 Phase D THE INDEPENDENCE EDGE (load-bearing): vlPFC (response selection/inhibition, Master S3) drives the volitional motor hub -- the regulation goal reaches the motor system. vlPFC is NOT in AFFECTIVE_EMPATHY, so the volitional route's drive is INDEPENDENT of the affective circuits; this is the only reason the dual-pathway dissociation (throttle affect, is the volitional route spared?) is measurable rather than unmeasurable by construction. SCAFFOLD band.

v14 Phase D THE VOCAL SUPPRESSOR: lateral premotor -> PAG-PANIC's local GABA gate (excitatory) -> net INHIBITION of PAG-PANIC's vocal output. The volitional suppressor of laughter/crying (Master S2.1; its loss = pathological laughing/crying). Band INHERITED byte-for-byte from the forebrain->PAG-GABA twin CeA->vlPAG-GABA (moderate-strong) -- not selected, so there is no degree of freedom to tune the suppression in. Matures school-age (online 5.0), so volitional cry-suppression emerges on schedule.

v14 Phase D the gate's inhibition of its column. Band INHERITED byte-for-byte from the twin vlPAG-GABA->vlPAG (strong, GABA-A). Present early (online 0.0, a subcortical gate); before the cortical suppressor matures it provides only weak tonic inhibition -- so the newborn cry is weakly volition-gated.

THE MAJOR EXCITATORY DRIVE TO LC (~73% of LC neurons driven; Aston-Jones 1986). Band `strong` from the literature's 'major' -- NOT chosen to dissolve the entailment (anatomy first, bands never). CeA->LC (moderate) is now the minor input it anatomically is.

PBN (parabrachial, lamina-I nociceptive/interoceptive relay) -> PGi, the LC integrator. Aston-Jones: PBN terminates ADJACENT to LC, not on it; PGi is the relay. Re-marks the WRONG-TARGET PBN->LC candidate.
NTS (visceral/cardiovascular afferent hub) -> PGi. Aston-Jones: NTS does not project to LC directly; PGi integrates it. Re-marks the WRONG-TARGET NTS->LC candidate.
Nociceptive drive to PGi -- PGi does pain processing (Aston-Jones 1986), so pain reaches LC through the PGi integrator (the exteroceptive arm of PGi's input).

v14 D6 fix #2 door 1 -- PGi's EXTEROCEPTIVE arm, the CITED edge (Van Bockstaele, Akaoka & Aston-Jones 1993, Brain Res 603:1-18; topographic companion). Dorsal PAG projects to the ROSTRAL/juxtafacial PGi 'medial to the facial nucleus' -- the LC-PROJECTING pole (l/vlPAG label the retrofacial pole instead, so the topography is real and the DORSAL column reaches the LC-projecting one). Creates a NON-AMYGDALA route from visual distress to the teaching signal: IN-VIS:biological_motion -> SC-Pv -> dPAG -> PGi -> LC (SC-Pv-> dPAG and PGi->LC already exist). Band `moderate` = the anatomical default, grounded FROM THE SOURCE, NOT chosen to dissolve the entailment. NB found by asking what the paper says is there, NOT what would dissolve the entailment -- the opposite epistemics from the refused SC-Pv->PGi (which the paper confirms is NOT a PGi afferent: the cited set is somatosensory/auditory/autonomic, no visual/collicular input).

v14 defensive-drive (A): MePD (GABAergic) -> the VMHvl shell -> DISINHIBITS the core. Replaces the AMPA-direct MeA->VMHvl (right outcome, wrong mechanism). Band inherited byte-for-byte from the twin CeA->vlPAG-GABA (moderate-strong). Net effect: MeA promotes aggression by disinhibition (MeA -| shell -| core).
v14 defensive-drive (A): the VMHvl shell inhibits its glutamatergic core. Band inherited byte-for-byte from the twin vlPAG-GABA->vlPAG (strong).

The RAW AUDITORY CHANNEL -- the analogue of IN-VIS->RET, added v14 (its own pass). Audition was the ONLY modality in the seed WITHOUT a raw input channel: vision has IN-VIS->RET, olfaction IN-OLF->OB, vestibular IN-VESTIB->VN, interoception IN-INTERO->NTS, but the only auditory input was IN-AUD:voice->A1-belt (a FEATURE channel landing in CORTEX), so COCH (the cochlear-nucleus brainstem entry) was completely unafferented and the whole subcortical pathway COCH->AUD-brainstem(IC)->SC-Pv/MGN + COCH->StN (startle) was dead (S42, the catalogue-vs-channels audit predicted it). This one edge lights the ascending auditory relay and lets STARTLE (PB-STARTLE: sound->COCH->StN->CeA) fire for the FIRST TIME in the model's existence, and carries auditory-THREAT salience to SC-Pv (which the escape paper says receives visual AND auditory threat). Textbook: cochlear nerve -> cochlear nuclei is the first central auditory relay. Band `moderate-strong`/anatomy, inherited from the IN-VIS->RET raw-channel template. NB the CRY specifically is still injected as IN-AUD:voice->A1-belt (cortex), so it BYPASSES this -- so door 1's auditory half needs the cry to also enter subcortically (a separate injection-depth fix); this edge fixes startle + generic auditory salience, not the cry's route. NEW BEHAVIOUR from one edge -- so if a startle test passes, ask what holds it (the freezing-floor lesson).

THE FREEZING LIMB -- the second clause of Tovote et al. 2016: vlPAG glutamatergic outputs project to PRE-MOTOR TARGETS IN THE MAGNOCELLULAR NUCLEUS OF THE MEDULLA, producing freezing. Twin of the vlPAG->NRA vocal limb (same source column, same strong/AMPA premotor projection); vlPAG IS the ventrolateral column. This edge closes the OUTPUT gap -- it does NOT close the freezing floor, because the DRIVE onto vlPAG-glut (VMH, low-moderate) is still weak (the driver question, measured separately). SCAFFOLD band.

Rozeske et al. 2018 (Neuron 97:898-910): a dmPFC subpopulation projecting to l/vlPAG controls contextual fear DISCRIMINATION -- optogenetic ACTIVATION -> discrimination (LESS freezing in the neutral context), INHIBITION -> generalisation (MORE freezing). SIGN FORCED BY THE BEHAVIOUR, not assumed: dmPFC is cortical -> glutamatergic -> excitatory; an excitatory source that produces LESS freezing CANNOT land on vlPAG-glut (that would drive freezing) -- it must EXCITE the GABAergic SELECTOR. So the synapse is EXCITATORY (AMPA) onto vlPAG-GABA, and the NET effect on the output is SUPPRESSION. The THIRD instance of the glutamatergic-source -> GABAergic-interneuron architecture (vlPFC->ITC, PMC-l->PAG-PANIC-GABA). It is the SELECTOR's third afferent, NOT the freezing driver -- the convenient candidate resolved into a different, correct thing (the dPAG->PGi pattern). A SECOND dmPFC->vlPAG arm exists (JCI 2022, rabies-transsynaptic): excitatory onto vlPAG-GLUT, descending ANALGESIA (vlPAG->RVM), NOT freezing -- BLOCKED: no RVM node exists; registered, not built. SCAFFOLD band.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- the drive that reaches CeM also RECRUITS the CeL inhibitory population, so CeL's inhibition scales with the drive it is gating.
connections.source: LUMP #13 selector build. The Stage-2 CEl drive limb, inherited BYTE-IDENTICAL onto BOTH selector populations -- deliberately SYMMETRIC so the microcircuit carries no built-in preference. Which population wins must emerge from the DIFFERENTIAL grounded afferents (LA->SOM+, PBN/OT->PKC-delta+), never from an asymmetry in the shared drive.

Which population wins must emerge from the DIFFERENTIAL grounded afferents (LA->SOM+, PBN/OT->PKC-delta+), never from an asymmetry in the shared drive.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: GABA-A INHIBITORY. ★ PROVENANCE DECONTAMINATED (audit finding): this field previously carried a COPY-PASTED scaffold justification ("set so the disynaptic feed-forward inhibition DOMINATES the residual direct PVN-OT->CeA fallback edge") describing an edge this one does not touch -- while the neighbouring source field simultaneously denied inheriting that scaffold. The record contradicted itself inside a single entry. The honest statement: this is a GABAergic CeL interneuron synapse, and its "strong" band is the literature's own qualifier for a competitive winner-take-all circuit, NOT a value carried over from the retired PVN-OT calibration.
connections.source: LUMP #13 selector build -- the WTA mutual inhibition (SOM+ -| PKC-delta+). LUMP #13 selector build -- WEIGHT GROUNDED FROM THE SELECTION-MICROCIRCUIT LITERATURE, NOT inherited from the retired 0.85 scaffold. The band is the literature's own qualifier: a COMPETITIVE/winner-take-all inhibitory circuit requires STRONG mutual inhibition (Fadok 2017), and the CeL->CeM feed-forward inhibition is characterised as the dominant CeM input (Ciocchi/Haubensak 2010). Note the number is no longer load-bearing the way it was: because the populations are mutually inhibitory, only ONE inhibits CeM at any moment, so a strong weight now ROUTES rather than CRUSHES. That is why the mechanism -- not a smaller value -- was the fix. | CITATIONS CORRECTED (independent fabrication audit, ruled). The mutual inhibition is REAL but Fadok 2017 was the wrong citation for THIS pair: Fadok's competing populations are CRF+ vs SOM+ (it never uses "SOM-negative" and never mentions PKC-delta). The SOM+/PKC-delta+ reciprocal inhibition is grounded instead by HAUBENSAK et al. 2010 ("PKC-delta+ neurons make reciprocal inhibitory synapses with PKC-delta- neurons in CEl") and LI et al. 2013 ("SOM+ and SOM- neurons in CeL mutually inhibit"). Fadok 2017 REMAINS correctly cited for the FREEZING half (SOM+ activation initiates passive freezing) and for the competitive active/passive selection ARCHITECTURE. APPROXIMATION NOW STATED RATHER THAN ASSUMED: SOM- is treated as PKC-delta+, which Li 2013 quantifies as LARGELY but not wholly non-overlapping (only 13 +/- 1% of SOM+ neurons express PKC-delta; the two together cover ~80% of CeL, so ~20% is neither). McCullough 2018 further reports Crf/Sst CO-LOCALISATION in CeL, so the flight-arm identity is an approximation, not an identity.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: OTR (Gq) EXCITATORY -- oxytocin excites CeA-lateral (CeL) GABAergic interneurons (Knobloch et al. 2012 Neuron 73:553). Weight 'strong' (# SCAFFOLD): OT strongly recruits CeL so the disynaptic inhibition dominates the direct edge -> net OT->CeA dampening (Knobloch 2012); silence-tested.
connections.source: LUMP #13 selector build. Oxytocin placed on PKC-delta+ SPECIFICALLY TO PRESERVE the measured Knobloch 2012 net effect: OT excites CeL and thereby REDUCES CeM output/freezing. PKC-delta+ is the population that inhibits the freezing output, so OT->PKC-delta+ reproduces "OT reduces freezing" mechanistically rather than by assertion. The v14 Phase 1.1 kinship result is carried forward, not disturbed. | ★ CIRCULAR JUSTIFICATION REPLACED WITH REAL GROUNDING (audit finding): this edge was justified as "placed SPECIFICALLY TO PRESERVE the measured Knobloch 2012 net effect" -- reverse-engineering from a wanted output. It is now grounded directly: HAN et al. 2015 reports PKC-delta overlapping the OXYTOCIN RECEPTOR (Oxtr) at ~65%, so OT acting on the PKC-delta+ population is an anatomical fact, not an inference from the effect it produces. Right answer, now for a real reason.

LUMP #13 selector build -- the SOM+ differential afferent. LA->CeL SOM+ is documented and is POTENTIATED BY FEAR CONDITIONING (Li et al. 2013, "experience-dependent modification of a central amygdala fear circuit"). One edge becoming two (LA->CEl stays), parallel to the ITC and BA splits. This is the conditioned/distal-threat drive to the passive-defence arm.

LUMP #13 selector build -- the PKC-delta+ differential afferent. The CGRP parabrachial projection targets CeL PKC-delta+ (CAI et al. 2014 -- CORRECTED: Han et al. 2015 defines its targets by CGRP-RECEPTOR expression and reports PKC-delta overlap as only modest and mainly caudal; the PKC-delta+ specificity is Cai et al. 2014, "~60% of LPB inputs to PKC-delta+ neurons derive from CGRP+ neurons"). One edge becoming two (PBN->CEl stays). This is the nociceptive drive to the active-coping arm, and together with LA->SOM+ it is what lets CONTEXT (rather than noise or a hardcoded rule) decide which population wins.
LUMP #13 selector build. The ruled BA->CEm excitatory drive, inherited BYTE-IDENTICAL onto both output populations -- the shared BLA drive the selector ROUTES between.
LUMP #13 selector build. ITC gates both CeM output populations, inherited byte-identical -- the prefrontal control route must reach both defensive modes, not one.
LUMP #13 selector build. The ruled BA->CEm excitatory drive, inherited BYTE-IDENTICAL onto both output populations -- the shared BLA drive the selector ROUTES between.
LUMP #13 selector build. ITC gates both CeM output populations, inherited byte-identical -- the prefrontal control route must reach both defensive modes, not one.

innate defensive backbone; Tovote et al. 2016. v14 Phase A -- TARGET CELL now explicit: CeA's GABAergic projection targets vlPAG GABAergic INTERNEURONS, which disinhibit the vlPAG glutamatergic output -> freezing. This is the mechanism the edge already cited and did not implement. The synapse is still INHIBITORY (GABA-A, now CITED rather than transmitter-fallback) and still 0.70 -- nothing about CeA's drive changed; only the target became explicit. central-amygdala threat/arousal drive of the locus coeruleus -- the aversive teaching-signal afferent (LC was UNAFFERENTED, so NA teaching was inert). Signed EXCITATORY via CRF-R1 (Gs-coupled): CeA is GABAergic, so its threat->arousal drive of LC is the CeA-CRF->LC projection acting on CRF-R1, not the GABA output (Van Bockstaele et al.). PHASIC/ADAPTING drive (phasic_drive): CRF release from CeA is phasic -- it fires on the event and adapts, it does NOT tonically clamp LC at CeA's plateau level. So LC is driven by CeA's DEVIATION above its running baseline (the mean_activity machinery this arc built for the teaching signal), not its absolute level. This is what makes the CeA<->LC loop unable to latch: at a sustained-high CeA plateau the phasic drive -> 0, so LC falls and CeA recovers (the tonic version latched CeA at saturation). Same tonic->phasic correction that fixed the teaching signal, applied to the drive itself. | LUMP #13 STAGE A: re-pointed from the CeA lump to CEm (the medial output division). connections.dominant_receptor_basis: GABA-A INHIBITORY. ★ PROVENANCE DECONTAMINATED (audit finding): this field previously carried a COPY-PASTED scaffold justification ("set so the disynaptic feed-forward inhibition DOMINATES the residual direct PVN-OT->CeA fallback edge") describing an edge this one does not touch -- while the neighbouring source field simultaneously denied inheriting that scaffold. The record contradicted itself inside a single entry. The honest statement: this is a GABAergic CeL interneuron synapse, and its "strong" band is the literature's own qualifier for a competitive winner-take-all circuit, NOT a value carried over from the retired PVN-OT calibration.
connections.source: LUMP #13 selector build -- CEl's LOCAL INHIBITION, the ruling's Stage-C deliverable (b). CeL SOM+/PKC-delta+ are LOCAL GABAergic interneurons: besides inhibiting each other and gating CeM, they inhibit CeL principal activity locally. This is the element whose ABSENCE Stage A measured as CEl saturating at 1.000 -- the old CeA-GABA->CeA local brake was re-pointed to CEm (correctly: its own provenance made it the CeL->CeM feed-forward edge), and nothing restored the local arm. It is also what the S56 Stage-2 loop closure was doing before the split, now in its properly DIFFERENTIATED form. Byte-identical to its twin (the same interneuron population, the same GABA-A synapse) -- no knob to tune. Without it the saturated CEl pumps maximal SYMMETRIC drive into both selector populations, which fights the winner-take-all and prevents the loser being suppressed. | CITATIONS CORRECTED (independent fabrication audit, ruled). The mutual inhibition is REAL but Fadok 2017 was the wrong citation for THIS pair: Fadok's competing populations are CRF+ vs SOM+ (it never uses "SOM-negative" and never mentions PKC-delta). The SOM+/PKC-delta+ reciprocal inhibition is grounded instead by HAUBENSAK et al. 2010 ("PKC-delta+ neurons make reciprocal inhibitory synapses with PKC-delta- neurons in CEl") and LI et al. 2013 ("SOM+ and SOM- neurons in CeL mutually inhibit"). Fadok 2017 REMAINS correctly cited for the FREEZING half (SOM+ activation initiates passive freezing) and for the competitive active/passive selection ARCHITECTURE. APPROXIMATION NOW STATED RATHER THAN ASSUMED: SOM- is treated as PKC-delta+, which Li 2013 quantifies as LARGELY but not wholly non-overlapping (only 13 +/- 1% of SOM+ neurons express PKC-delta; the two together cover ~80% of CeL, so ~20% is neither). McCullough 2018 further reports Crf/Sst CO-LOCALISATION in CeL, so the flight-arm identity is an approximation, not an identity.

LUMP #13 / S56 EXIT -- the grounded EXCITATORY driver of the freezing column (RULED). The prelimbic cortex drives conditioned fear EXPRESSION (infralimbic SUPPRESSES it), and mPFC sends a dense glutamatergic projection to the ventrolateral PAG column. dmPFC is the seed's own prelimbic-analogue ("dorsomedial PFC / prelimbic-analogue", function "Sustained-threat attention; fear expression"), so this is PL->vlPAG, NOT IL. WHY THIS EDGE AND NOT A CeM ONE, measured: the CeM->PAG freezing pathway is ALREADY built as CEm-freeze->vlPAG-GABA (Tovote 2016, disinhibitory) and the diagnosis proved it delivers NOTHING -- under threat that gate already sits at 0.000, and silencing it entirely changes vlPAG by zero. A disinhibitory route cannot deliver drive when the gate it opens is already open. A DIRECT CEm-freeze->vlPAG edge could not fix this either: CeM projection neurons are GABAergic (verified independently in the S59 fallback-sign census -- "CeA->HYPdm is genuinely INHIBITORY, CeM output neurons are GABAergic"), so a direct edge would SUPPRESS the column, backwards. The column needs actual EXCITATION, and PL is where the literature puts it. NOTE THE DEFECT THIS ALSO CORRECTS: dmPFC's only route to the column was dmPFC->vlPAG-GABA, i.e. the model's fear-EXPRESSION node was driving the freezing BRAKE -- directionally backwards for PL. That edge is RETAINED (PL does modulate the gate) and this direct excitatory arm is ADDED alongside it. Band inherited BYTE-IDENTICAL from that twin (moderate, AMPA, same basis/confidence) -- no knob to tune.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: AMPA (glutamatergic) EXCITATORY -- the drive that reaches CeM also RECRUITS the CeL inhibitory population, so CeL's inhibition scales with the drive it is gating.
connections.source: LUMP #13 CLOSE. The CEl drive limb, inherited BYTE-IDENTICAL from both siblings (CEl->CEl-SOM and CEl->CEl-PKCd) so all three CeL populations receive the SAME shared drive. Any preference must come from the differential afferents, never from a thumb on the scale in the shared limb.

connections.dominant_receptor_basis: GABA-A INHIBITORY. ★ PROVENANCE DECONTAMINATED (audit finding): this field previously carried a COPY-PASTED scaffold justification ("set so the disynaptic feed-forward inhibition DOMINATES the residual direct PVN-OT->CeA fallback edge") describing an edge this one does not touch -- while the neighbouring source field simultaneously denied inheriting that scaffold. The record contradicted itself inside a single entry. The honest statement: this is a GABAergic CeL interneuron synapse, and its "strong" band is the literature's own qualifier for a competitive winner-take-all circuit, NOT a value carried over from the retired PVN-OT calibration.
connections.source: LUMP #13 CLOSE -- THE FADOK COMPETITIVE CIRCUIT (Fadok et al. 2017, Nature 542:96). CRF+ and SOM+ are MUTUALLY INHIBITORY CeL populations, and this reciprocal inhibition IS the selection mechanism: which population wins determines active (CRF+ -> flight) vs passive (SOM+ -> freezing) defence. Both directions byte-identical to the existing SOM+/PKC-delta+ mutual inhibition -- same cell class, same GABA-A synapse, same literature-qualified "strong" band for a competitive winner-take-all. No knob to tune. The selection must EMERGE from which population the drives favour, never from an asymmetry built into these weights.

connections.source: LUMP #13 CLOSE -- the CRF+ output to the ACTIVE-DEFENCE column. Fadok 2017 reports CRF+ neurons projecting widely throughout the PAG columns, including the dorsolateral/lateral (flight/escape) columns; dPAG is the model's active-coping column (v14 Phase A vlPAG=passive / dPAG=active). SIGN GROUNDED, and it is DISINHIBITORY for the same reason the freeze arm is: CeL populations are GABAergic, so CRF+ reaches the column by inhibiting its GABAergic gate (dPAG-GABA), releasing dPAG. ★ AND UNLIKE THE FREEZE ARM, THERE IS SOMETHING BEHIND THIS GATE: dPAG carries real excitatory drive (SC-Pv looming, moderate; VMHvl attack, strong), whereas vlPAG had only the non-threat-driven VMH -- which is exactly why vlPAG needed PL excitation and this column does not. The disinhibition-without-drive defect was checked for before building, not after. FLAGGED, NOT GUESSED: Fadok also reports CRF+ -> VMH, but the flight-relevant target is VMHdm (dorsomedial, predator defence) and the model has no VMHdm -- its VMH is reproductive/social and its VMHvl is the attack area. That projection is therefore NOT built rather than mapped onto a mismatched node.

substrate_hardening_backlog: S56 -- the nine-node gate-family calibration (DRN-GABA/vlPAG-GABA/dACC-GABA/dlPFC-GABA/vmPFC-GABA/CeA-GABA/ITC/PAG-PANIC-GABA/VMHvl-GABA share one brake band+baseline regardless of afferent load; dlPFC saturates to 1.0). ★ HIGHEST PRIORITY: the ONLY thing between the current state and a fully-green suite -- it gates BOTH the freezing-floor red AND the divergence xfail; grounding it is predicted to return both green. The question: should an E-I loop's inhibition scale with drive, and does ours? (mechanism-design, family-wide).

the dl/l dPAG SPLIT -- VMH->vlPAG's correction condition (VMHdm/c immobility belongs on the dorsolateral column, fused into dPAG); which VMH population, how dl vs l differ, whether VMHvl->dPAG re-points. On the split, ground VMH->vlPAG's band to STRONG at the right target (currently strong but on the wrong column).
the OPIOID system (4 circuits name opioids in prose, none exists; dense mu-opioid on vlPAG-GABA; shared with PAG-PANIC's soothing arm -- one circuit, two registers; system-scale diagnosis).
the LUMP census (12 known lumps incl. vlPAG 4-transmitter; MeA's SPLIT-BLOCKED edges MeA->MPOA/MeA->BNST; PBN subnuclei lateral/medial/Kolliker-Fuse). Feeds the 209-connection audit.
the S65 basis:assumption census (provenance sibling of the fallback-sign census; one-line query; may reframe 'grounded connectome' the way Q1 reframed 'grounded baselines').

the INPUT-SURFACE gaps: PB-LOOMING (the escape trigger the model cites and cannot fire), predator odor (blocks the MeA split), PB-STARTLE, PR-SALT, PR-HOMEOSTATIC, IN-PROPRIO (S23, no agency claim until it lands).

the SS18 systemic widening (master doc + thesis limitations): the one-number baseline layer (S61), the nine-node gate scaffold, effect-sizes-biased-upward (measured law), cortical brake layer, typing-order hazard (S44), Form-3-is-a-class. The 47 assumption-based connections + these feed the 209-connection audit (a later scoped STAGE, not backlog to chase).
